A mist of sleet began to fall as the horizon lightened . It looked to Leo as if they were on a slab of tableland with nothing in their way for a day or two until the river breaks . Riding in the rear he was ten or twelve rods from Duwayne , but often they ranged close enough to talk . Chief was half way up the herd on the south flank , with Cobb and the others too far ahead to see .
一阵冻雨般的雾开始降落，地平线开始变亮。在莱奥看来，他们似乎处在一个台地的平台上，前方一两天内都没有障碍，直到河流中断。他骑在队伍后面，距离杜韦恩有十到十二杆远，但经常靠得足够近可以交谈。酋长在牛群南侧大约一半的位置，而科布和其他人则太靠前了，看不见。

source: Duwayne hooted . " You bet ! " he said . " Some swell in fancy britches is gon na slap his little pancake saddle on that ? He pointed to a skinny , hammer-headed gray mare , her loose belly hanging , thick winter hair sticking at angles and scabbed with snow , and one chewed ear cocked wrong . She was n't much worse than her nearest companions . All of them seemed unusually small to Leo .
domestication: 杜韦恩大笑起来。“那当然！”他说，“这瘦骨嶙峋的灰骒马，头大得像锤子，肚子松垮垮地挂着，冬天的厚毛乱七八糟，还沾着雪，一只耳朵被咬得歪歪扭扭。谁会在这上面放个小鞍子啊？”他指着那匹马说。这马比旁边那些也强不了多少。在莱奥眼里，这些马都显得特别小。
foreignization: 杜韦恩吼了一声。“你打赌！”他说。“有个穿花哨裤子的阔佬要在那匹马上放他的小马鞍吗？”他指着一匹瘦削、锤头状的灰母马，她的松弛的肚子垂挂着，厚厚的冬季毛发杂乱无章地竖立着，上面结了雪痂，一只被咬过的耳朵歪斜着。她并不比她的近邻更糟糕。在利奥看来，它们都显得格外矮小。

source: Leo found that none of this came as a surprise . But yet he thought that surely there was some market for these culls . Knudsen was n't sending them off with only a hope and prayer : he could n't be fool enough to expect any horsetrader in the country to pay more than a nickel for these woeful goats .
domestication: 莱奥发现这一切都不出他所料。但转念一想，这些劣马总该有点销路吧。克努森可不是抱着一线希望和祷告就把它们打发走的：他不可能傻到指望国内哪个马贩子会为这些可怜的家伙掏出一个子儿。
foreignization: 莱奥发现这一切并不让他感到意外。但他认为，肯定有人会买这些劣等货。克努森不会仅仅抱着希望和祈祷就把它们送出去：他不可能愚蠢到期望这个国家的任何一个马贩子会为这些可怜的山羊出价超过五分钱。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Leo found that none of this came as a surprise . But yet he thought that surely there was some market for these culls . Knudsen was n't sending them off with only a hope and prayer : he could n't be fool enough to expect any horsetrader in the country to pay more than a nickel for these woeful goats .

source: On their ponies , they moved in search of the herd . The wind descended In furious swirls , seeming to throw a woolly skirt of snow around them wherever they moved , as if they were the eye of the storm . Cautiously and together they moved toward where the herd might be , and when Buster floundered into a dark flank that leaped away , they decided they had found it . With Chief 's lariat joining their two saddles at twenty feet , they rode back and forth along a thirty rod line . As far as Leo could tell no horses tried to get past .

source: For a moment he stood looking down at the stupefied face , the blistering cheeks , the white-tipped nose , on which the snow melted as it fell . This guy was nearly a grown man , older than Leo by three or so years , a lot further along the road . If this is where the wisdom come of his experience took him , God help the sorry lot of us , thought Leo . None of it made a picture that he saw himself in except at the blurred edge , where the artist had gotten careless . He could hear himself saying in his head , to hell with you , Duwayne , which he would n't say aloud : but it echoed in there .
domestication: 他愣了一下，低头看着那张呆滞的脸，脸上烧得通红，鼻尖上顶着一片白，雪花刚落上去就化了。这人快成大人了，比里奥大个三岁左右，已经走得老远了。如果他的人生智慧和经验把他带到这种地步，天哪，我们这些可怜人可怎么办，里奥心想。这画面里，只有边缘模糊的地方，画家画得随意了些，他才勉强看到自己的影子。他心里默念着：见你的鬼去吧，杜韦恩，虽然他嘴上没说，但这声音在脑子里回荡着。

source: Cobb would n't leave the herd , or whatever part of it he had found . He was frantic that they 'd never get out of this storm in decent shape -- in any shape and already he had lost no telling how many : enough for his old man to skin him alive . Dis-tractedly he listened to about half of Leo 's story and then yelled , " The son of a bitch 'll have to catch us , that 's all ! Let him find his damn horse ! " His shrill voice popped out of the oval into which his hood was drawn , just space for his eyes and nose . Chief , listening beside him , wheeled his pony toward the herd , and disappeared .
domestication: 科布不愿意离开牛群，哪怕只是找到的一部分。他急得像热锅上的蚂蚁，生怕他们无法安全地走出这场风暴——简直连命都保不住了。他已经不知道丢了多少头牛，这下子回去肯定被老爹活剥了皮。心不在焉地听莱奥讲了一半的故事，他突然大喊：“那混蛋想抓我们，就让他来试试！让他去找他的破马吧！”他的声音尖锐，从斗篷的兜帽里挤出来，只露出了眼睛和鼻子。酋长在他旁边听着，忽然掉转马头朝牛群奔去，眨眼间就不见了踪影。
foreignization: 科布不会离开牛群，或者他找到的那部分牛群。他焦急万分，担心他们永远无法体面地——甚至以任何形式——走出这场风暴，而且他已经不知道损失了多少头牛：足够让他老爹活剥了他的皮。心不在焉地听了利奥的故事大概一半，然后他大喊：“那个混蛋只能自己追上我们，就这样！让他去找他的该死的马吧！”他尖锐的声音从兜帽拉成的椭圆形小口中蹦出，只露出眼睛和鼻子的空间。酋长在他旁边听着，掉转马头朝向牛群，然后消失了。

Cobb would n't leave the herd , or whatever part of it he had found . He was frantic that they 'd never get out of this storm in decent shape -- in any shape and already he had lost no telling how many : enough for his old man to skin him alive . Dis-tractedly he listened to about half of Leo 's story and then yelled , " The son of a bitch 'll have to catch us , that 's all ! Let him find his damn horse ! " His shrill voice popped out of the oval into which his hood was drawn , just space for his eyes and nose . Chief , listening beside him , wheeled his pony toward the herd , and disappeared .
科布不愿意离开牛群，哪怕只是找到的一部分。他急得像热锅上的蚂蚁，生怕他们无法安全地走出这场风暴——简直连命都保不住了。他已经不知道丢了多少头牛，这下子回去肯定被老爹活剥了皮。心不在焉地听莱奥讲了一半的故事，他突然大喊：“那混蛋想抓我们，就让他来试试！让他去找他的破马吧！”他的声音尖锐，从斗篷的兜帽里挤出来，只露出了眼睛和鼻子。酋长在他旁边听着，忽然掉转马头朝牛群奔去，眨眼间就不见了踪影。

source: He could see no reason for it , but his pony seemed suddenly to step out with new purpose . Leo felt a moment of hope although there was nothing to support it . Indeed , it seemed gloomier around him , dirty gray beyond the swirling snow . It could be nightfall coming on , he thought , or some darker dimension of the storm working itself up .
domestication: 他看不出个所以然，但他的小马忽然间像是有了新目标，迈开了步子。莱奥心头闪过一丝希望，尽管这希望毫无凭据。周围似乎更加阴沉了，雪花纷飞中透出脏兮兮的灰色。他心想，可能是夜幕降临了，或者风暴正在酝酿出更黑暗的一面。
foreignization: 他看不出有什么理由，但他的小马似乎突然间迈着新的目标前行。莱奥感到一丝希望，尽管没有任何东西可以支撑这种希望。事实上，他周围的环境似乎更加阴郁，雪花飞舞之外是一片肮脏的灰色。他想，可能是夜幕降临了，或者风暴正在酝酿出某种更黑暗的维度。

He could see no reason for it , but his pony seemed suddenly to step out with new purpose . Leo felt a moment of hope although there was nothing to support it . Indeed , it seemed gloomier around him , dirty gray beyond the swirling snow . It could be nightfall coming on , he thought , or some darker dimension of the storm working itself up .
他看不出有什么理由，但他的小马似乎突然间迈着新的目标前行。莱奥感到一丝希望，尽管没有任何东西可以支撑这种希望。事实上，他周围的环境似乎更加阴郁，雪花飞舞之外是一片肮脏的灰色。他想，可能是夜幕降临了，或者风暴正在酝酿出某种更黑暗的维度。

source: With a handful of dead leaves dried in his hand inside his mitten -- his old man had done that once with oak leaves -- he brought a small flame to life . The sight of it buoyed him beyond measure , and he crouched over it to keep the snow off . Out of his saddle-pouch he dug the food sack , not knowing what he 'd find -- whatever Cobb had handed him that he had n't passed on to Duwayne before being hauled away in Cobb 's panic . It held a large twist of flour , a hand-sized slab of bacon and two cans of tomatoes .... No coffee .
domestication: 手里戴着厚手套，握着一把枯叶——他老爹以前也用橡树叶子这么干过——他生起了一小簇火苗。看到火光，他的心一下子暖了起来，蹲下身护住火苗，不让雪花扑灭。从马鞍包里翻出食物袋，也不知道里面有什么——科布给的，还没来得及递给杜韦恩，就慌慌张张地被抓走了。袋子里有大团面粉，一块巴掌大的咸肉，还有两罐番茄……就是没有咖啡。

With a handful of dead leaves dried in his hand inside his mitten -- his old man had done that once with oak leaves -- he brought a small flame to life . The sight of it buoyed him beyond measure , and he crouched over it to keep the snow off . It held a large twist of flour , a hand-sized slab of bacon and two cans of tomatoes .... No coffee .

With a handful of dead leaves dried in his hand inside his mitten -- his old man had done that once with oak leaves -- he brought a small flame to life . The sight of it buoyed him beyond measure , and he crouched over it to keep the snow off . Out of his saddle-pouch he dug the food sack , not knowing what he 'd find -- whatever Cobb had handed him that he had n't passed on to Duwayne before being hauled away in Cobb 's panic . It held a large twist of flour , a hand-sized slab of bacon and two cans of tomatoes .... No coffee .
手里握着一把枯叶——他老爹曾经用橡树叶子做过一次——他在手套里把它们弄干，然后点燃了一小簇火苗。看到火光让他无比振奋，他蹲下来，以防积雪扑灭火焰。从马鞍袋里，他掏出了食物袋，不知道里面有什么——科布给他的东西，他在被科布慌乱地带走之前还没来得及交给杜韦恩。袋子里有一大卷面粉、一块手掌大小的培根和两罐西红柿……没有咖啡。

source: In his cup he melted snow , lamenting the lack of coffee , and into it mixed enough flour to make a batter . This he fried in his lard-can pan , after the slab of bacon he had first sliced into it gave up its fat , snow flakes sizzling as they hit the hot grease . The batter bubbled up into the drippings and hardened . He let the dough soak up the grease , then wrapped the halfcooked bacon in it . He now had what the old man had called grust , and when Leo asked him why , he said because that was the way it looked , did n't it ? It warmed his insides , but , Goddamn , would n't coffee go over good now ?
domestication: 他在杯子里融了雪，心里却抱怨着没有咖啡，又往里头掺了足够的面粉，调成了面糊。他先切了几片培根丢进铁锅，等培根熬出了油，再把面糊倒进去，雪花一碰到热油，就发出滋滋的响声。面糊在油里冒泡，慢慢变硬。他让面团充分吸收油分，然后把半熟的培根包在里面。这样，他就做出了老头说的那种“格鲁斯特”。莱奥问他为什么这么叫，他说这不是挺像的嘛？这东西暖了肚子，可是，老天爷，现在要是能来杯咖啡该有多好啊！
foreignization: 他在杯子里融化了雪，哀叹没有咖啡，然后往里面混入足够的面粉，做成面糊。他先在lard-can平底锅里切了一块培根，等培根释放出油脂后，将面糊倒入锅中，雪花碰到热油时发出嘶嘶声。面糊在油脂中起泡并变硬。他让面团吸收油脂，然后将半熟的培根包在里面。现在他有了老人所说的grust，当莱奥问他为什么这么叫时，他说因为看起来就是这样，不是吗？它暖了他的胃，但，该死的，现在来点咖啡该多好啊？

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

In his cup he melted snow , lamenting the lack of coffee , and into it mixed enough flour to make a batter . This he fried in his lard-can pan , after the slab of bacon he had first sliced into it gave up its fat , snow flakes sizzling as they hit the hot grease . The batter bubbled up into the drippings and hardened . He let the dough soak up the grease , then wrapped the halfcooked bacon in it . He now had what the old man had called grust , and when Leo asked him why , he said because that was the way it looked , did n't it ? It warmed his insides , but , Goddamn , would n't coffee go over good now ?
他在杯子里融了雪，心里却抱怨着没有咖啡，又往里头掺了足够的面粉，调成了面糊。他先切了几片培根丢进铁锅，等培根熬出了油，再把面糊倒进去，雪花一碰到热油，就发出滋滋的响声。面糊在油里冒泡，慢慢变硬。他让面团充分吸收油分，然后把半熟的培根包在里面。这样，他就做出了老头说的那种“格鲁斯特”。莱奥问他为什么这么叫，他说这不是挺像的嘛？这东西暖了肚子，可是，老天爷，现在要是能来杯咖啡该有多好啊！

In his cup he melted snow , lamenting the lack of coffee , and into it mixed enough flour to make a batter . This he fried in his lard-can pan , after the slab of bacon he had first sliced into it gave up its fat , snow flakes sizzling as they hit the hot grease . The batter bubbled up into the drippings and hardened . He let the dough soak up the grease , then wrapped the halfcooked bacon in it . He now had what the old man had called grust , and when Leo asked him why , he said because that was the way it looked , did n't it ? It warmed his insides , but , Goddamn , would n't coffee go over good now ?
他在杯子里融化了雪，哀叹没有咖啡，然后往里面混入足够的面粉，做成面糊。他先在lard-can平底锅里切了一块培根，等培根释放出油脂后，将面糊倒入锅中，雪花碰到热油时发出嘶嘶声。面糊在油脂中起泡并变硬。他让面团吸收油脂，然后将半熟的培根包在里面。现在他有了老人所说的grust，当莱奥问他为什么这么叫时，他说因为看起来就是这样，不是吗？它暖了他的胃，但，该死的，现在来点咖啡该多好啊？

" Leo could have told him stories he 'd heard about starving ponies eating tarpaper off the roof of a shack , and others nibbling on sheeps ' wool .

source: He slept some , for short stretches , but kept coming awake unable to feel his toes and wriggling them until he could . But then he thought , hell , he did n't know but what Chief and Cobb had nailed down the herd and come back to build a nice camp by Duwayne 's rock , and they were all chuckling around a nice fire , drinking coffee , and Leo was the only one who did n't know where ; he was ....

source: Uncovering the bits of firewood he had saved , he lighted a tiny fire and stood over it pinching his nose until he could feel it . It was no use trying to punch open a can of frozen tomatoes , so he thawed a thumb of bacon and melted a cupful of snow for a drink . The idea of coffee sat large in his mind as he sipped the warm water , scarcely better than nothing . But along with the bacon it set him up sufficiently to see some small chance in the day ahead -- if there were no more storms .
domestication: 他掀开藏好的柴火，点起了一小堆火，然后站在那儿捏着鼻子，直到感觉到了火的暖意。想用拳头砸开冻成冰块的西红柿罐头根本不可能，于是他只能先烤化一小截培根，再融一壶雪水解渴。虽然这温水喝起来和没喝差不多，但他心里还是想着咖啡。好在培根加上这口水也勉强能撑一阵子，让他觉得这一天还有一点盼头——只要不再来一场风暴。
foreignization: 揭开他保存的木柴，他点燃了一小堆火，站在那儿捏着鼻子直到他能感觉到它。试图用拳头打开一罐冻番茄是没有用的，所以他解冻了一拇指厚的培根，并融化了一杯雪作为饮料。当他喝着温水时，咖啡的想法在他的脑海中占据了很大的位置，这水几乎和什么都没喝一样。但与培根一起，这足以让他在即将到来的一天中看到了一些渺茫的机会——前提是不再有风暴。

But along with the bacon it set him up sufficiently to see some small chance in the day ahead -- if there were no more storms .

It might be the best way to go , he thought . But as he turned Buster this way and that he saw the slope he was on could only take him east . domestication: 到了上午早些时候，他已从溪谷底爬了上来，远远高过了昨晚的宿营地。他停下来，回头望去，只见风吹雪在每一个低洼处迅速堆起，心里不禁疑惑自己是不是走错了方向，正往来的路上走去，朝他们留下杜韦恩的地方。或许这样最好，他想。但当他左右转着巴斯特时，发现所处的斜坡只能带他向东。那边还有一道山脊，他打赌到了山顶就能看到畜群留下的痕迹，一条大大的、乱糟糟的雪路……

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

By early fore-noon he had climbed out of the creek bottom and was high above and beyond his night 's camp . When he stopped to look back across the strings of blowing snow rapidly building drifts in every declivity , he wondered whether he 'd gotten turned around and was heading back the way he 'd come , toward where they had left Duwayne . It might be the best way to go , he thought . But as he turned Buster this way and that he saw the slope he was on could only take him east . Another ridge loomed In that direction and he bet himself that on the top of it he 'd see the herd 's trail , a big messy road in the snow ....

source: Nothing looked right in all this snow . In his head he knew how this country was supposed to look , and it did n't look that way . Cobb was meant to be headed onto flat land , plains , a gentle slope a man could coast down all the way to Illinois , not anywhere close to these hog-backs and cliffs and ravines and shoulderbone ridges . This was no landscape where you would expect to see men , Leo thought . One man sure did n't amount to anything here -- or ten , or a hundred .
domestication: 这雪地里啥都不对劲。他心里清楚这片土地该是啥样，可眼前的景象却完全不对头。按理说，科布应该是要前往一片平坦的土地，一望无际的平原，或是缓缓倾斜的山坡，人可以一路滑到伊利诺伊州，而不是这种满是山脊、峭壁和沟壑的地方。这样的地势，压根儿就不适合人类生存，利奥心想。在这儿，一个人根本算不了什么——哪怕十个人，甚至一百个人，也无济于事。
foreignization: 在这片雪地中，什么看起来都不对劲。在他脑海中，他知道这个国家应该是怎样的景象，而眼前并不是那样。科布本应前往平坦的土地，平原，一个可以一路滑行到伊利诺伊州的温和斜坡，而不是这些脊状山岭、悬崖、峡谷和肩骨山脊。这并不是一个你会期待看到人的地方，利奥想。在这里，一个人确实算不了什么——或者十个人，或一百个人。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Nothing looked right in all this snow . In his head he knew how this country was supposed to look , and it did n't look that way . Cobb was meant to be headed onto flat land , plains , a gentle slope a man could coast down all the way to Illinois , not anywhere close to these hog-backs and cliffs and ravines and shoulderbone ridges . This was no landscape where you would expect to see men , Leo thought . One man sure did n't amount to anything here -- or ten , or a hundred .

source: As he pondered the difficulty of plowing his way to it , he was startled to see a dark figure emerge from the white mist of snow to the east . He could hardly restrain himself from yelling , but then realized he might only be hollering at an old bull buffalo , or one of the horses spun off Cobb 's herd . But in another minute a second figure took on shape behind the first , and soon several more followed .
domestication: 他正苦思冥想着如何才能闯过去，突然间，东边的雪雾中出现了一个黑影，把他吓得不轻。他差点儿就要喊出来了，但又想到可能是只老野牛，或者是科布那群马里跑出来的一匹。可没过一分钟，第二个身影出现在了第一个身后，接着又出现了好几个。
foreignization: 当他思索着如何艰难地向那里前进时，他突然看见一个黑影从东边的雪白雾气中出现。他几乎忍不住要大喊，但随即意识到那可能只是一头老野牛，或是科布的马群中跑出来的一匹马。但又过了一分钟，第二个身影在第一个后面显现出来，很快又有几个身影跟上。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

As he pondered the difficulty of plowing his way to it , he was startled to see a dark figure emerge from the white mist of snow to the east . He could hardly restrain himself from yelling , but then realized he might only be hollering at an old bull buffalo , or one of the horses spun off Cobb 's herd . But in another minute a second figure took on shape behind the first , and soon several more followed .

As he pondered the difficulty of plowing his way to it , he was startled to see a dark figure emerge from the white mist of snow to the east . He could hardly restrain himself from yelling , but then realized he might only be hollering at an old bull buffalo , or one of the horses spun off Cobb 's herd . But in another minute a second figure took on shape behind the first , and soon several more followed .
当他思索着如何艰难地向那里前进时，他突然看见一个黑影从东边的雪白雾气中出现。他几乎忍不住要大喊，但随即意识到那可能只是一头老野牛，或是科布的马群中跑出来的一匹马。但又过了一分钟，第二个身影在第一个后面显现出来，很快又有几个身影跟上。

" You better make sure your dog there does n't chase after our puck , " one of the kids growled , and needless to say , neither of the captains chose me .

I stood a little away from the other kids by the side of the ice , trying to ignore my little sister and hoping to get chosen for the hockey game . As the new kids in town , Susan and I might as well have been outlaws for the attention we received . " You better make sure your dog there does n't chase after our puck , " one of the kids growled , and needless to say , neither of the captains chose me .
我站在冰场边，离其他孩子有点远，尽量不理会我妹妹，心里盼着能被选上打曲棍球。我和苏珊作为镇上的新来者，受到的关注让我们感觉就像外星人一样。“你最好管好你的狗，别让它追我们的球，”一个孩子凶巴巴地说。不用说，两队的队长都没选我。

source: This was not the first time I 'd returned to Venice . In fact , hers were shores I could n't keep from returning to , a place where I could always come to float and disappear for a while -- half labyrinth , half harbor , safely buffetted from both tides and time . Adding up all the days and months , I 'd passed nearly three years of my life there , though of course it 's ridiculous to quantify time in Venice . To try and fix a number to a page or talk in terms of calendars . Drift along any of her murmuring canals late of an evening and you 'll see what I mean . Aside from the occasional light bulb , you could be in any century .
domestication: 这已经不是我第一次回到威尼斯了。其实，这里是我总也离不开的地方，每次都能来这儿漂浮一会儿，消失一阵子——半是迷宫，半是港湾，既能避开潮水，也能逃离时间的侵蚀。算起来，我在那儿的日子加起来几乎有三年了，不过在威尼斯，时间这东西根本无法量化，用数字或日历来衡量更是荒唐。晚上沿着那些低语的运河漫游，你就能明白我的意思了。除了偶尔几盏电灯，你会感觉自己仿佛置身于任何一个世纪。
foreignization: 这已经不是我第一次回到威尼斯了。事实上，她的海岸是我无法不重返的地方，一个我可以总是来漂浮和消失一阵子的地方——一半是迷宫，一半是港口，安全地隔绝了潮汐和时间。算上所有的日月，我在那里度过了将近三年的生命，尽管当然，用数字来衡量在威尼斯的时间是荒谬的。试图给一页纸定个数字或用日历来说话。傍晚时分沿着她低语的运河漂流，你就会明白我的意思。除了偶尔出现的灯泡，你可能会觉得置身于任何一个世纪。

source: I climbed the stairs , flight after flight , growing heavier and breathless with each step . Finally I reached the upper floor , where their apartment door stood ajar . The thought raced through my head that I could still run . I could still ... the sound of utensils from within , whispers . I pushed open the door and stepped inside . An umbrella leaned against the wall , a pair of shoes nearby without laces . In the kitchen , bowls of minestrone sat steaming on the table beneath upturned faces . "
domestication: 我爬了一层又一层的楼梯，每上一步都感觉身体更沉重，喘不过气来。终于，我到了顶楼，他们家的门半开着。我脑子里闪过一个念头：还能跑，还能……从屋里传来碗筷的声音和细语声。我推开门，走了进去。墙边斜靠着一把伞，旁边是一双没系鞋带的鞋。厨房里，餐桌上的意大利面汤正冒着热气，几张脸朝向我转了过来。
foreignization: 我爬了一层又一层的楼梯，每走一步都感到越来越沉重，喘不过气来。终于到达了上层，他们的公寓门半开着。一个念头飞快地闪过我的脑海，我还可以逃跑。我还可以……从里面传来了餐具的声音，还有低语声。我推开了门，走了进去。一把雨伞靠在墙上，旁边是一双没有系鞋带的鞋子。在厨房里，汤锅里的意式蔬菜汤正冒着热气，桌子下面是一张张仰起的脸。"

I climbed the stairs , flight after flight , growing heavier and breathless with each step . Finally I reached the upper floor , where their apartment door stood ajar . The thought raced through my head that I could still run . I could still ... the sound of utensils from within , whispers . I pushed open the door and stepped inside . An umbrella leaned against the wall , a pair of shoes nearby without laces . In the kitchen , bowls of minestrone sat steaming on the table beneath upturned faces . "

source: I had a brother once , or almost did . He came and went before I was born , so we never actually met . Never shared any actual time together face-to-face in a room . Indeed I did n't even learn about him until I was nearly eleven , when my mother sat me down in the kitchen one evening . I remember our dirty dinner plates were still on the table . I remember carrots , too . The kind that came cubed in a can . The way they still sat in a clump at the edge of my plate , pushed aside and cold .
domestication: 我曾经有个弟弟，或者说差点就有了。他在我出生前就来了又走了，所以咱们俩从没见过面，也没在同一个房间里面对面地待过。其实，直到快十一岁的时候，我才知道有这么一回事。那是一个晚上，妈妈把我叫到厨房里跟我说的。我还记得当时餐桌上的盘子还没洗，都是吃剩下的。还有那些罐头里的胡萝卜丁，我用筷子夹到一边，凉了也没吃。

I remember carrots , too . The kind that came cubed in a can . The way they still sat in a clump at the edge of my plate , pushed aside and cold .

I had a brother once , or almost did . He came and went before I was born , so we never actually met . Never shared any actual time together face-to-face in a room . Indeed I did n't even learn about him until I was nearly eleven , when my mother sat me down in the kitchen one evening . I remember our dirty dinner plates were still on the table . I remember carrots , too . The kind that came cubed in a can . The way they still sat in a clump at the edge of my plate , pushed aside and cold .
我曾经有一个弟弟，或者说几乎有过。他在我出生前就来了又走了，所以我们实际上从未见过面。从未在同一个房间里面对面地共度过任何时间。事实上，直到我快十一岁的时候，母亲才在一个晚上把我叫到厨房里，我才了解到他。我还记得我们的脏餐盘还在桌子上。我也记得有胡萝卜。那种来自罐头的切成立方体的胡萝卜。它们还聚成一堆在我的盘子边缘，被推开后已经变冷了。

source: There are all kinds of amputations and oversights in this world . All kinds of cripples . The water accepts them all without reservation , yet the solid world has little tolerance . Even Venice . At some point in the past that gardener must have been careless , inattentive . Just as Alessandro had been in that moment he shared a needle . Just as I was being careless by way of being too careful . Careful in friendships , love , you name it . Always keeping my measured distance , staving off not only loss , but life in the process .
domestication: 这世上的残缺和疏忽各式各样，什么样的残疾人都有。水都无条件地接纳他们，但坚实的大地却容不得多少，就连威尼斯也不例外。过去的某个时候，那个园丁肯定也有过疏忽大意的时候。就像亚历山大在共用针头的那一刻疏忽了，也像我因为太过小心而粗心了一样。在友情、爱情上，我总是小心翼翼，保持一定的距离，这样既能避免失去，但也失去了生活本身。
foreignization: 这个世界上有各种各样的残缺和疏忽。各种各样的残废。水无条件地接纳了它们，然而固体世界却很少容忍。即使是威尼斯也是如此。在过去某个时候，那个园丁一定曾经粗心大意，注意力不集中。就像亚历山德罗在共用针头的那个时刻一样。就像我因为过于小心而变得粗心大意。在友谊、爱情等方面都很小心。总是保持一定的距离，不仅避免了失去，也避免了生活本身。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

There are all kinds of amputations and oversights in this world . All kinds of cripples . The water accepts them all without reservation , yet the solid world has little tolerance . Even Venice . At some point in the past that gardener must have been careless , inattentive . Just as Alessandro had been in that moment he shared a needle . Just as I was being careless by way of being too careful . Careful in friendships , love , you name it . Always keeping my measured distance , staving off not only loss , but life in the process .

source: Toward dusk we went indoors to feed ourselves . Meals with Rico were always simple -- cooked whole potatoes or rice , handfuls of clementines and dried figs , bananas , dates -- food plucked from the same crates that fed his animals . Rico ate with fingers half bent , like the monkeys themselves . A 27-inch television sat beside the table , which Rico kept constantly playing for a voice . Usually we 'd sit together and watch , now and then Rico talking back to the screen , cursing at all the politicians , the commercials , the Pope . That evening though , I got a hold of the remote control and found the mute switch .

Toward dusk we went indoors to feed ourselves . Meals with Rico were always simple -- cooked whole potatoes or rice , handfuls of clementines and dried figs , bananas , dates -- food plucked from the same crates that fed his animals . Rico ate with fingers half bent , like the monkeys themselves . A 27-inch television sat beside the table , which Rico kept constantly playing for a voice . Usually we 'd sit together and watch , now and then Rico talking back to the screen , cursing at all the politicians , the commercials , the Pope . That evening though , I got a hold of the remote control and found the mute switch .

source: " However , I 'm convinced that if they place you in the ground there 'll be worms born . Little worms , without eyes or legs . Who knows what kind of life they lead , underground the way they are . You see them , you do n't see them . There are machines that can sense such things , but I do n't know ... but I am certain that if someone could follow such a worm that one day a bird would come and eat it and then shit you back down to the ground to fertilize the trees . Or else carry you back to their nest and feed their young so that one day you can fly . Either way , you join all the rest . The animals , the trees , the air , the water ... this is another thought , more than death . "
domestication: 不过，我确信，要是把你埋在土里，肯定会有小虫子生出来。那些小虫子，没眼睛没腿的。谁晓得它们地下过的是什么日子呢。你瞧不见它们，它们却实实在在地活着。有些机器能探测到这些，但我也不清楚……不过我敢肯定，要是有人能跟着这么一条小虫子，总有一天会有一只鸟把它吃掉，然后拉屎时又把你拉回地上，给树当肥料。或者，那鸟儿把你带回巢里喂小鸟，等小鸟能飞了，你也算是飞起来了。反正，到最后，你总归是跟其他的一切融为一体了。动物啊，树木啊，空气啊，水啊……这比死亡更让人想不通。
foreignization: “但是，我确信如果你被埋在地下，就会有蚯蚓出生。小蚯蚓，没有眼睛或腿。谁知道它们过着怎样的生活，在地下。你看到它们，又看不到它们。有机器可以感知这些东西，但我不知道……但我确定，如果有人能跟随这样的蚯蚓，总有一天一只鸟会来吃它，然后把你拉回地下施肥给树木。或者把它带回巢里喂养幼鸟，这样有一天你就能飞了。不管怎样，你都加入了其他所有生物。动物、树木、空气、水……这是一种超越死亡的思考。”

source: Still , it struck me that despite the distance there was a bridge between the boat and my eye . I could see it and it could see me , or if not me specifically , at least the shore . For though a vessel 's prow may yearn for the open waves its stern must watch the shore recede . And even if it were to drift out beyond sight , I still knew it was there just as the boat would still be connected to the shoreline , for there is no being at sea without a shore . There is no amputation then . Memory , not only a shipwreck , but a harbor to return to again and again . A shoebox . A photograph . A face frozen in time . A crippled heart waiting to beat anew . There is no loss without gain , I thought . There is no loss in truth , only gain .
domestication: 尽管相隔遥远，我还是觉得船和我的眼睛之间仿佛有座桥相连。我能看到它，它也能看到我，或者至少能看到岸。因为，船头虽然向往着广阔的波涛，但船尾却得盯着渐渐远去的海岸。即便它漂到了看不见的地方，我也知道它还在那里，船也始终与岸边有着千丝万缕的联系，因为没有离开大海就没有海岸。所以，没有什么是真正失去的。记忆不仅是一场海难，更是一个可以一次次回去的港湾。一个鞋盒，一张照片，一张定格在时光里的脸庞，一颗等待重新跳动的心。我想，有失必有得。事实上，根本没有什么真正的失去，只有获得。
foreignization: 仍然，我感到尽管有距离，船和我的眼睛之间还是有一座桥。我能看见它，它也能看见我，或者至少看见岸边。因为虽然船头可能向往着开阔的波浪，但船尾必须看着海岸线渐渐远去。即使它漂移到视线之外，我还是知道它在那里，正如船依然与海岸线相连，因为没有海岸就谈不上在海上。因此，不存在截肢。记忆，不仅是沉船，而且是一个可以一次又一次返回的港口。一个鞋盒。一张照片。一张定格在时间里的脸。一颗等待重新跳动的残破的心。没有失去就没有获得，我想。事实上，只有获得，没有失去。

Still , it struck me that despite the distance there was a bridge between the boat and my eye . I could see it and it could see me , or if not me specifically , at least the shore . For though a vessel 's prow may yearn for the open waves its stern must watch the shore recede . And even if it were to drift out beyond sight , I still knew it was there just as the boat would still be connected to the shoreline , for there is no being at sea without a shore . There is no amputation then . Memory , not only a shipwreck , but a harbor to return to again and again . A shoebox . A photograph . A face frozen in time . A crippled heart waiting to beat anew . There is no loss without gain , I thought . There is no loss in truth , only gain .

仍然，我感到尽管有距离，船和我的眼睛之间还是有一座桥。我能看见它，它也能看见我，或者至少看见岸边。因为虽然船头可能向往着开阔的波浪，但船尾必须看着海岸线渐渐远去。即使它漂移到视线之外，我还是知道它在那里，正如船依然与海岸线相连，因为没有海岸就谈不上在海上。因此，不存在截肢。记忆，不仅是沉船，而且是一个可以一次又一次返回的港口。一个鞋盒。一张照片。一张定格在时间里的脸。一颗等待重新跳动的残破的心。没有失去就没有获得，我想。事实上，只有获得，没有失去。

source: Joseph Brodsky has written that to leave Venice always feels as if you are leaving it forever : " For leaving is a banishment of the eye ... And to the eye , for purely optical reasons , departure is not the body leaving the city but the city abandoning the pupil ... A tear is the anticipation of the eye 's future . "
domestication: 约瑟夫·布罗茨基曾写道，离开威尼斯总是让人感觉像永别：“因为离开就是眼睛的流放……对于眼睛来说，由于纯粹的光学原因，离开不是身体离开了城市，而是城市抛弃了瞳孔……一滴眼泪，是眼睛对未来的预感。”

source: Her first ball ! She was only at the beginning of everything It seemed to her that she had never known what the night was like before . Up till now it had been dark , silent , beautiful very often -- oh , yes -- but mournful somehow . Solemn . And now it would never be like that again -- it had opened dazzling bright .
domestication: 她的第一次舞会！她才刚刚开始这一切呢。她觉得以前从不知道夜晚是怎样的。直到现在，夜总是黑暗的，寂静的，常常也很美——哦，没错——但总带着几分哀伤，庄重。可从今往后再也不会那样了——因为今晚它变得如此耀眼夺目。

" No , " she said , " I wo n't go outside . I wo n't sit down . I 'll just stand here , thank you . " She leaned against the wall , tapping with her foot , pulling up her gloves and trying to smile . But deep inside her a little girl threw her pinafore over her head and sobbed . Why had he spoiled it all ?
“不，”她说，“我不出去，也不坐下，就站这儿，谢谢。”她倚着墙，脚尖轻轻敲打着地面，拉了拉手套，努力挤出一丝微笑。可心底里，那个小女孩却扯过围裙蒙住了头，啜泣起来。他干吗要毁了这一切呢？

source: Just as the rise in ten-year-old blood sugar threatens mass delirium , State Park Ranger Ariel arrives with a guitar , camp songs , and ghost stories . He leads us on a night walk , shines a flashlight on annoyed owls , and points out the Big Dipper . source: Though Benjy is overtired , I let him stay up to watch the Winter Olympics . We chew pizza through a report on the Slovakian luger who recovered from brain surgery and won the bronze . They broadcast a long stretch of the Canada vs . Finland hockey game , but no one scores . When the women 's figure skating finals come on , I perk up . The fifteen-year-old Korean favorite performs nearly flawlessly but then falls hard during her last triple toeloop . An athletic Russian completes all her technical elements , but the transitions are sloppy , and her scores reflect her lack of artistry . A low-ranking Swiss pixie impresses the judges with solid jumps and crisp footwork , and she unexpectedly moves into first place .
domestication: 本杰明虽然困得不行，我还是让他熬夜看冬奥会。我们边看斯洛伐克雪橇运动员脑部手术后复出夺铜的报道，边嚼着披萨。接着播了加拿大对芬兰的冰球赛，可是双方都没进球。当女子花样滑冰决赛开始时，我精神一振。十五岁的韩国小甜心几乎完美地完成了她的表演，可是在最后一个三周跳时重重摔倒了。一位俄罗斯选手技术动作完成得不错，但衔接部分粗糙，艺术表现力也欠佳，分数自然不高。一个名不见经传的瑞士小精灵，以稳健的跳跃和干净利落的步伐赢得了裁判的青睐，意外地跃居首位。

It is dearly Clare 's practiced look of public seriousness . My guess now is that well never see inside No. 61 , never get to appreciate all the disabled addons . source: " Let 's grab some air , " Clare says , and roughly , with his bony left fist , begins hauling me up the dune behind us , our shoes sinking in sand , my recovered balance a bit pitched forward , my neck and back breaking a warm sweat . " Maybe you got vertigo , " Clare says as he guys me up toward the dune top , his long legs doing the work for two . " Men our age get that . It goes away . "
domestication: “咱们出去透透气吧。”克莱尔说着，用他那只瘦骨嶙峋的左手用力地把我拽上身后的沙丘，我们的鞋子深陷在沙子里，我好不容易恢复了平衡，却有点前倾，脖子和后背都冒出了细汗。“可能是你有点恐高。”克莱尔一边拉我往沙丘顶上走，一边说，他的长腿像是替两个人使力。“像我们这把年纪的人常有的，过一阵子就好了。”

source: " Who knows how old anybody is anymore , Frank ? " We 're now at the top . The steely-blue , flat-surfaced Atlantic is beyond the wide low-tide beach . A smudge of gray-brown crud hangs just at the horizon . In the middle distance , a boatful of day fishermen is anchored , their short poles abristle off both sides . The small breeze seems to blow straight through my ears and brings a shiver that makes me understand that for late November I 'm dressed too lightly . Of course , I believed I 'd be inside by now . " I look at twenty-five-year-olds and then somebody tells me they 're fifteen , " Clare natters on . " I look at thirty-five-year-olds who look fifty . I give up . "
domestication: “弗兰克，现在谁还知道谁多大呢？”我们已经到了顶上。眼前是宽阔的退潮海滩，再远处是铁蓝色、平坦如镜的大西洋。地平线上挂着一条灰褐色的污痕。中距离的地方，一艘载着日间垂钓者的船锚泊着，两边短杆林立。微风直穿耳际，带来一丝寒意，让我意识到自己十一月底穿得实在太少了。本来以为这时候已经在屋里了。“我看那些二十五岁的，结果人家告诉我才十五。”克莱继续唠叨，“我看三十多岁的，却像五十多岁。我算是服了。”
foreignization: “谁知道现在任何人多大了，弗兰克？”我们现在在顶部。钢铁蓝、表面平坦的大西洋在宽阔的退潮海滩之外。一抹灰褐色的污物悬挂在地平线上。中距离处，一艘满载日间垂钓者的船锚定在那里，他们的短杆从两边竖立着。微小的微风似乎直吹过我的耳朵，带来一阵让我明白在十一月底我穿得不够暖和的寒意。当然，我以为我现在会在里面。“我看着二十五岁的人，然后有人告诉我他们十五岁，”克莱喋喋不休地说。“我看着三十五岁的人却像五十岁。我放弃了。”

source: " Vertigo , " Clare says again , and gives his large , overly helical ear a good stern grinding with the heel of his hand . He clearly likes being up here on the dune , as anyone would . It 's approximately the view one would get from a " reimagined " floor three of the big-but-compromised Doolittle house behind us . Maybe his mind 'll change now . domestication: “眩晕，”克莱尔又说了一遍，然后用手掌根部狠狠地揉了揉他那大而卷曲的耳朵。他显然很喜欢待在这沙丘上，谁不喜欢呢。从这里看过去，景色大概就像我们身后的杜立特大屋重新设计过的三层楼一样。也许现在他的想法会改变。也许破败的柱子也没那么烦人了。换个角度看问题，事情就会有所不同。

I think a house on the ocean 's a good thing . Then I start to think about New Jersey being a target for some nut with a bomb or whatever . domestication: “这就轮到我出场了，弗兰克。”克莱尔说，“这可是我心里一直打转的念头。我想让埃斯特莉的晚年过得开心些。我觉得海边的房子不错。接着我又开始担心新泽西会不会成为某个疯子的目标，放个炸弹什么的。当然了，我知道死亡其实很简单。见过，不害怕。我知道埃斯特莉可能比我先走。但直到现在，我还是一直在看房子，好像灾难或死亡不会真的发生似的。可到了最后，不得不承认它们确实可能发生。然后我就吓呆了，真是动弹不得。”

“我想你不会想到的，”克莱尔说。“你为什么要想呢？我非常清楚这都和埃斯特尔生病，我的另一段关系结束，还有我的年龄有关。我只是害怕我的生活状况会变得糟糕。砰砰” 克莱尔仍然凝视着大海，越过那些苗条、完美且完全无忧无虑的年轻排球手们的头顶——一个饱经风霜的老麦哲伦，不喜欢他终于发现的东西。砰砰。

source: My friend 's friend lived on the farm next door to my grandfather 's house . I ca n't believe that my grandfather beat their dog . source: I get a letter from my mother . She sends a picture . In the picture grandfather is sitting on the lawn in front of his house . He has a wreath of dandelions on his head . A group of laughing children are sitting around him . I recognize all of them . I am in the picture , too . My mother has enclosed a short note . She writes , " I found the picture in a shoebox , I had forgotten all about it , and think you should have it . "
domestication: 我收到了妈妈的来信，还有一张照片。照片里，爷爷正坐在他家前院的草地上，头上戴着一顶蒲公英花环。一群笑嘻嘻的孩子围坐在他身边，我一个个都认得出来，我也在其中。妈妈还在信里附了一段话：“我在一个鞋盒里找到了这张照片，差点都忘了有这事儿了，觉得你应该收着。”
foreignization: 我收到母亲的来信。她寄了一张照片。照片里祖父坐在他房子前的草坪上。他的头上戴着一串蒲公英。一群笑着的孩子围坐在他身边。我认出了他们所有人。照片里也有我。母亲附了一张短笺。她写道，“我在一个鞋盒里找到了这张照片，我差点忘了这件事，觉得你应该拥有它。”

source: He sits at the dining table with the checked oilcloth . He mashes the overcooked potatoes into the sauce . He continues until it is a sleek brown mash , then he lifts his fork with a little mashed potato to his mouth . He swallows with great difficulty and has to wash it down with milk . The clock strikes once , he turns his thin face to me and says , " I have such a small throat that I ca n't swallow a pea . " We both look at the peas on his plate . Later he goes out to the kitchen and spills the peas into the dog 's bowl . The fat black dog hurries to the bowl and eats .
domestication: 他坐在铺着格子油布的餐桌旁，把煮得烂糊的土豆捣进酱汁里。捣啊捣，直到变成了一滩光滑的棕色泥。然后他用叉子挑起一小块土豆泥，送到嘴边，艰难地吞了下去，还得喝口牛奶才顺得下去。钟敲了一下，他转过瘦削的脸对我说：“我的嗓子眼儿太细了，连颗豌豆都咽不下去。”我们俩一起盯着他盘子里的豌豆看。后来，他去厨房，把豌豆倒进了狗碗里。那只肥黑的狗立马跑过来，津津有味地吃了起来。

source: I told him about Operation Berkshire , a sting operation of a few decades ago , which was the subject of my writing project . In this part of New England , a group of hunters had been poaching black bears and selling their gall bladders to middlemen , who in turn sold them in Asia for use as aphrodisiacs and intestinal medicine . For two years , two environmental cops posed as rogue hunters and became part of the gang . Then one winter day , twenty-five hunters were simultaneously arrested in four states at six o'clock in the morning .
domestication: 我跟他讲了伯克希尔行动的事，这是一桩几十年前的卧底行动，也是我写作项目的内容。在新英格兰的这一带，有一群猎人偷猎黑熊，把熊胆卖给中间商，这些熊胆最终被卖到亚洲，当作壮阳药和治肠胃病的药。环保警察乔装成不守规矩的猎人，潜伏在他们中间，一干就是两年。后来在一个冬日的清晨六点，四州同时行动，一举逮捕了二十五名猎人。
foreignization: 我告诉他关于“伯克希尔行动”，这是一次发生在几十年前的诱捕行动，也是我的写作项目的主题。在新英格兰的这一地区，一群猎人一直在非法猎杀黑熊，并将熊胆卖给中间商，后者再将熊胆销往亚洲，用于春药和肠道药物。在两年的时间里，两名环保警察伪装成违法猎人并成为了这个团伙的一部分。然后在一个冬天的早晨，二十五名猎人在四个州同时被逮捕，时间是早上六点。

For two years , two environmental cops posed as rogue hunters and became part of the gang . Then one winter day , twenty-five hunters were simultaneously arrested in four states at six o'clock in the morning .

source: Clay and I made a plan to go over to the shooting range . Smith - Wesson offered a " Massachusetts Carry Permit Course " covering the safety and legalities of firearms ' use , and a few basics of shooting . Upon completion , I would get a certificate , enabling me to march down to my local police station and apply for a Class A unrestricted gun license . Although Clay liked being my guide , a class might be good so I would n't take too much of his time .
domestication: 我和克莱约好一起去靶场。史密斯-韦森提供了一个“麻省持枪许可课程”，涵盖了枪支使用的安全和法律知识，还有一些基本的射击技巧。学完后，我就能拿到证书，然后大摇大摆地去当地警察局申请一个A类无限制枪证。虽然克莱乐意当我的向导，但上个课也不错，这样不会占用他太多时间。
foreignization: 克莱和我制定了一个去射击场的计划。史密斯-韦森提供了一个“马萨诸塞州携带许可课程”，涵盖了枪支使用的安全和法律问题，以及一些基本的射击技巧。完成课程后，我将获得一张证书，使我能够前往当地的警察局申请A类无限制枪支许可证。虽然克莱喜欢做我的向导，但上一堂课可能也不错，这样我就不会占用他太多时间。

source: We walked down the hall to the shooting range . I held the pistol . I loaded it . I shot the gun . Clay advised me not to think about the target . Just get comfortable holding the gun . Always point it down-range . Never have your finger on the trigger until you are going to shoot . The intense effort to focus on nothing but shooting was singularly pleasurable . source: " What are you gon na do ? What 's the word ? No ! Wrong word ! Do n't say Kill . Say Stop him . If you have an avenue of escape , you must use it . I 'm thirty feet from you , threatening you with this knife , but you are next to the back door there . You can leave . But what if I 'm moving toward you which he does , wielding the knife and there is no door ? What about chairs , what about these tables ? Use a table as shield . The knife ca n't go through the table . And verbalize . Even if you do n't see anyone , someone might be watching . This is the Third Eye Concept . Always verbalize . Drop the f-ing knife ! That 's what you have to say ! "
domestication: 你要怎么办？怎么说？不行！说错了！别提“杀”字，要说“阻止他”。要是有路可逃，一定要走。我拿着刀站在你三米开外威胁你，但你旁边就是后门，你可以离开。但如果我朝你走过来，挥着刀，又没有门可以逃怎么办？想想椅子，想想桌子。可以用桌子当盾牌。刀子穿不透桌子。还要大声喊出来。就算你看不见人，也可能会有人在看。这是“第三只眼”的概念。任何时候都要大声说出来。“放下该死的刀！”你必须这么说！

source: Jim uses all of us as characters in every story he enacts . This is good entertainment , but of course , entertainment is the least of it . After a few hours , I do n't know how I can continue being so focused . It 's hard to be part of his dramas for eight hours straight . At one point Jim points to me , and says " She 's been raped and murdered . " I do n't know what he says after that , because I am wondering why on earth I am even there in this moment . The moment passes .
domestication: 吉姆在每个故事里都把我们当成角色来演。这确实挺好玩的，但好玩只是其中一小部分。几个小时下来，我真不知道自己是怎么坚持下来的。连续八个小时参与他的戏码，真是不容易。有次，吉姆指着我说：“她被强奸了，还被杀了。”之后他说了什么我都没听进去，满脑子都在想自己怎么会在这里受这份罪。那股劲儿过去了，我也就缓过来了。
foreignization: 吉姆在每次表演的故事中都把我们所有人当作角色。这是很好的娱乐，但当然，娱乐只是其中最少的一部分。几个小时后，我不知道我怎么能继续保持如此专注。连续八个小时参与他的戏剧真的很困难。在某个时刻，吉姆指着我说：“她被强暴和谋杀了。”我不知道他之后说了什么，因为我在想为什么我竟然会在那一刻出现在那里。那一刻过去了。

source: I have had a long-time fascination with querencia . Querencia is the invisible place in a bull-ring where the bull goes during a bullfight , his place of safety where he gathers strength and becomes fearless . I have always had an overdeveloped need for querencia , for sanctuary . The need to protect one 's territory is common in humans and other animals .
domestication: 我对“夸伦西亚”这个词一直很着迷。夸伦西亚是斗牛场上那片无形的地方，公牛会在斗牛时躲到这里，这里是它的安全地带，它在这里积蓄力量，变得无所畏惧。我一直特别需要这么一个夸伦西亚，一个避风港。保护自己的领地，这在人和动物中都很常见。
foreignization: 我一直对querencia有着浓厚的兴趣。Querencia是在斗牛场中肉眼看不见的地方，斗牛在这里可以聚集力量并变得无所畏惧。我一直以来都有强烈需要querencia，需要庇护所。保护自己领地的需求在人类和其他动物中都很常见。

source: It is now test time . I am exhausted . Jim passes out the test . By now he has told us all the questions and answers . Some of us have taken notes which we can keep out during the test . Under these conditions , anyone can pass it . Jim leaves the room . When he returns , he corrects the papers . Everyone has passed . In a few minutes , we will all have our certificates . The day is over , we say goodbye , and I drive home to the Berkshires .
domestication: 现在开始考试了，我累得不行。吉姆开始发试卷，这会儿他已经把所有的问题和答案都告诉我们了。有些同学还做了笔记，考试时可以拿出来看。这么一来，谁都能轻松过关。吉姆出了门，等他回来的时候，批改了我们的卷子。结果，大家全过了。再过一会儿，我们每个人都能拿到证书了。一天就这么过去了，大家道了再见，我开车回家，回伯克希尔山去了。
foreignization: 现在是测试时间。我精疲力尽。吉姆分发测试卷。到目前为止，他已经告诉我们所有的问题和答案。我们中的一些人做了笔记，可以在测试期间保留这些笔记。在这种情况下，任何人都能通过。吉姆离开了房间。当他回来时，他批改了试卷。所有人都通过了。几分钟后，我们都会拿到证书。一天结束了，我们道别，我开车回家去伯克郡。

source: The following week , I set out for the police station to apply for a gun permit . There was a chill in the air , snow predicted for the next day . I walked up through the basketball courts , past the red plastic jungle gym , and noticed a toy sign Ambulance affixed to it . How odd to have a sign implying calamity in a children 's playground . I rehearsed what I would say to the Chief of Police when he interviewed me . Jim had said that when we applied for a Class A Unrestricted Permit , we would have to make a strong case for why we needed a handgun for self-protection more than the average citizen . Because I took the course to qualify , I wanted to apply .
domestication: 下个星期，我动身去了警察局申请枪证。那天空气里透着一股寒意，预报说明天会下雪。我穿过篮球场，路过那个红色的塑料游乐设施，注意到上面挂着一个“救护车”的玩具标志。儿童乐园里出现这种暗示灾难的东西，真是奇怪。我在心里琢磨着等会儿面见警长时该怎么说。吉姆说过，申请A类无限制持枪证时，我们得强有力地证明自己比一般人更需要手枪自卫。因为我参加了资格课程，所以想试试申请。

下一周，我出发前往警察局申请枪支许可。空气中透着寒意，预报说明天会下雪。我穿过篮球场，经过红色塑料的游乐设施，注意到上面固定着一个玩具标志“救护车”。在儿童游乐场里有暗示灾难的标志，真奇怪。我练习着当警察局长面试我时我要说的话。吉姆说过，当我们申请A类无限制许可证时，我们必须有力地说明为什么我们需要手枪自卫的需求比普通公民更强烈。因为我参加了资格课程，所以我想申请。

source: Yesterday , driving over to Smith - Wesson with Clay , I called him my shooting buddy , then changed it to shooting teacher . " I 'm your shooting sensei , " he said with his Southern drawl . He sees shooting as a martial art with all the discipline , focus , and meditative activity such undertakings require . Later I looked up sensei .
domestication: 昨天和克莱开车去史密斯-韦森，我先叫他我的射击伙伴，后来改口叫他射击老师。“我是你的射击师傅。”他用南方口音说道。他把射击看作是一门武术，需要全神贯注、严守纪律，还要有冥想的成分。后来我查了查“师傅”这个词的意思。

source: Clay asks me to repeat the steps over and over . Push the lower button in to release the magazine . When the magazine slides out , hold it in your left hand and insert the ammo . One at a time , pop five shells into the magazine with your right hand . Each shell is called a round . Clay loads only five rounds at a time , although the magazine holds ten , to keep track of what he has already shot . Each time you raise your gun , you shoot a string of five rounds .
domestication: 克莱让我一遍又一遍地重复这些步骤。按下下方的按钮，弹匣就会弹出来。弹匣滑出后，用左手握住它，然后右手一次装一发子弹，一共装五发。每一发子弹叫一发弹。虽然弹匣能装十发，但克莱每次只装五发，好知道自己打了多少。每次举起枪，就是连射五发。
foreignization: Clay 让我一遍又一遍地重复这些步骤。按下底部按钮以释放弹匣。当弹匣滑出时，用你的左手握住它并装入弹药。一次一个，用你的右手将五发子弹装入弹匣。每发子弹被称为一发。Clay 每次只装五发，尽管弹匣可以容纳十发，以便追踪他已经射击过的数量。每次你举起枪时，你会射出五发子弹的连发。

source: Now that I have my gun license , now that I have spent my long day at Smith &; Wesson , now that I have been able to talk about my shooting to men on the right and wrong sides of the law , now that I 've told friends and family about all this and heard their various reactions , now that I have had the sensation of shooting a pistol , now that I have joked about using my government issued gun permit at the airport - what now ? Am I really going to learn how to shoot ? Would I pack a pistol and go hiking in the woods ? Am I going to own a gun ?
domestication: 现在我有了枪证，现在我在史密斯威森枪厂度过了漫长的一天，现在我和黑白两道的人都聊过我的射击经历，现在我和亲朋好友分享了这一切，听到了他们的各种反应，现在我体验了开手枪的感觉，现在我还拿我的政府发的枪证在机场开玩笑——接下来该怎么办？我真的要去学怎么开枪吗？我会带着枪去森林里远足吗？我真的要拥有一把枪吗？

source: The house looks inside much like Brewster 's apartment always looked - comfortable , squared away - although that may be Susan 's doing . There 's a long counter , a serving bar between the kitchen and dining room , and arrayed on top are five or six of the fabric-covered boxes that Brewster made , along with a camera and his antique pistol . " Do you want that ? " Susan asks when she notices me notice it . " Take anything you want , really . " " No , but thanks , " I say .
domestication: 这房子里面看起来跟布鲁斯特的公寓一模一样，舒服又整洁，这可能得归功于苏珊。厨房和餐厅之间有个长长的吧台，上面摆着五六个布鲁斯特做的布面盒子，还有一台相机和一把老式手枪。“你要这个吗？”苏珊见我盯着这些东西看，就问我。“随便拿，喜欢什么就拿什么。”“不用了，谢谢。”我说。
foreignization: 这所房子里面看起来很像布劳斯特的公寓一贯的样子——舒适、整洁——尽管这可能是苏珊的功劳。有一个长长的台面，厨房和餐厅之间有一道服务吧台，上面摆放着五六只布劳斯特做的布面盒子，还有一台相机和他的老式手枪。“你要那个吗？”苏珊注意到我注意到了，便问道。“真的，随便拿什么都可以。”“不了，不过谢谢。”我说。

The house looks inside much like Brewster 's apartment always looked - comfortable , squared away - although that may be Susan 's doing . There 's a long counter , a serving bar between the kitchen and dining room , and arrayed on top are five or six of the fabric-covered boxes that Brewster made , along with a camera and his antique pistol . " Do you want that ? " Susan asks when she notices me notice it . " Take anything you want , really . " " No , but thanks , " I say .
这房子里面看起来跟布鲁斯特的公寓一模一样，舒服又整洁，这可能得归功于苏珊。厨房和餐厅之间有个长长的吧台，上面摆着五六个布鲁斯特做的布面盒子，还有一台相机和一把老式手枪。“你要这个吗？”苏珊见我盯着这些东西看，就问我。“随便拿，喜欢什么就拿什么。”“不用了，谢谢。”我说。

" Do you want that ? " Susan asks when she notices me notice it . " Take anything you want , really . " " No , but thanks , " I say .
这所房子里面看起来很像布劳斯特的公寓一贯的样子——舒适、整洁——尽管这可能是苏珊的功劳。有一个长长的台面，厨房和餐厅之间有一道服务吧台，上面摆放着五六只布劳斯特做的布面盒子，还有一台相机和他的老式手枪。“你要那个吗？”苏珊注意到我注意到了，便问道。“真的，随便拿什么都可以。”“不了，不过谢谢。”我说。

domestication: 这些版画都是铜版凹印的。它们让人联想到日本画家所说的“寂”这种感觉。他们认为有八种超凡脱俗的孤独场景，一遍又一遍地描绘。其中一个场景是一群大雁在田野上空盘旋，总是笼罩着一层薄雾，画面定格在领头雁刚刚触地的那一刻。
foreignization: # EADY（旁白）# 这些版画是铜板凹版画。它们唤起了一种日本画家称为“ sabi ”的感觉。他们相信有八个场景表现出超凡的孤独。他们一遍又一遍地描绘这些场景。其中一个场景是一群雁在田野上空盘旋。总是有雾。它被画成正是领头雁触地的时候。

source: # CEZAR # A bank . On Thursdays it gets cash deposits for distribution to other branches . domestication: CEZAR是一家银行。每周四，它会接收现金存款，然后分发到其他分行。这些分行得增加手头的现金，好应付周五丰田、日产工厂、钢铁厂和两家炼油厂的工资支票。每周就这一天，周四，主分行得扛起这全部的压力。

source: twisted in her seat , trying to see , frightened by the noise and sirens . WIDEN . An L.A.P.D. fire engine ROARS in . Now an unmarked car skids into the driveway . Drucker , Bosko and others spill out carrying shotguns . A black and white parks perpendicular to the traffic flow and halts cars . She 's worried , confused .
domestication: 她在座位上扭来扭去，想看看外面，却被噪音和警报声吓得不轻。镜头拉远，一辆洛杉矶消防车呼啸而至。紧接着，一辆无牌照的车也猛地滑进车道，德雷克、博斯科和其他人从车上跳下来，手里举着散弹枪。一辆黑白警车横停在路中间，拦住了过往的车辆。她又担心又迷茫。

source: It was beastly hot weather , as Juniper remarked to Captain Lysander out on the veranda of the officers ' mess , fit for little more than an odd gin and tonic , perhaps the lazy composition of a letter home . The flies , maddening creatures that had never learned to take no for an answer , crowded around the nets that blocked the porch , searching for a way in . " I would trade a hand to be back in London , " Lysander said to Juniper after a long pause . " At least they have the decency to bar these flies from coming into the city there . "
domestication: 天气热得要命，杜松子在军官食堂的门廊上对莱桑德上尉说，这种天气除了喝杯金汤力，顶多再懒洋洋地给家里写封信，别的啥也不想干。那些苍蝇真是烦人，从不识趣，围在挡着门廊的网子周围，寻思着怎么钻进来。“我真想把手换回伦敦去，”莱桑德停了好一会儿才对杜松子说，“至少在那里，城里把这些讨厌的苍蝇拒之门外。”
foreignization: 天气热得要命，正如杜松对莱桑德上尉在军官食堂的门廊上所说，除了偶尔喝一杯金汤力，也许懒洋洋地给家里写封信之外，几乎什么都做不了。那些令人发狂的苍蝇，这些从未学会接受拒绝的生物，挤在阻挡门廊的网子周围，寻找着进入的方法。“我愿意用一只手换回在伦敦的日子，”莱桑德在长时间的沉默后对杜松说。“至少在那里，他们有礼貌地不让这些苍蝇进入城市。”

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

It was beastly hot weather , as Juniper remarked to Captain Lysander out on the veranda of the officers ' mess , fit for little more than an odd gin and tonic , perhaps the lazy composition of a letter home . The flies , maddening creatures that had never learned to take no for an answer , crowded around the nets that blocked the porch , searching for a way in . " I would trade a hand to be back in London , " Lysander said to Juniper after a long pause . " At least they have the decency to bar these flies from coming into the city there . "
天气热得要命，杜松子在军官食堂的门廊上对莱桑德上尉说，这种天气除了喝杯金汤力，顶多再懒洋洋地给家里写封信，别的啥也不想干。那些苍蝇真是烦人，从不识趣，围在挡着门廊的网子周围，寻思着怎么钻进来。“我真想把手换回伦敦去，”莱桑德停了好一会儿才对杜松子说，“至少在那里，城里把这些讨厌的苍蝇拒之门外。”

Translate the following text to Chinese.

The flies , maddening creatures that had never learned to take no for an answer , crowded around the nets that blocked the porch , searching for a way in . " I would trade a hand to be back in London , " Lysander said to Juniper after a long pause . " At least they have the decency to bar these flies from coming into the city there . "
天气热得要命，正如杜松对莱桑德上尉在军官食堂的门廊上所说，除了偶尔喝一杯金汤力，也许懒洋洋地给家里写封信之外，几乎什么都做不了。那些令人发狂的苍蝇，这些从未学会接受拒绝的生物，挤在阻挡门廊的网子周围，寻找着进入的方法。“我愿意用一只手换回在伦敦的日子，”莱桑德在长时间的沉默后对杜松说。“至少在那里，他们有礼貌地不让这些苍蝇进入城市。”

source: Heading home at 5:00 p.m. after his tour had ended , he surveyed the road . The trooper didn'twrite a lot of tickets here , leaving that to the county deputies -- professional courtesy -- but he occasionally lit up somebody in a German or Italian car if he was in a mood , and this was the route he often took home at this time of day , so he knew the highway pretty well .
domestication: 五点钟下班后，他开车回家，路上四下张望。这路段的交警不常开罚单，那些活儿通常留给县里的警员——大家都是同行，得给点面子嘛。不过要是心情不好，偶尔还是会找那些开德国车或意大利车的司机麻烦。这条路是他下班回家的常走路线，所以对这一带的路况了如指掌。
foreignization: 下午5:00巡完逻后回家，他环视着道路。这里的交警不开很多罚单，把这事留给县里的副警官——这是职业上的相互尊重——但他偶尔会心情好时对开德国车或意大利车的人开张罚单，而这条路是他经常在这个时间回家的路线，所以他很熟悉这条高速公路。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Heading home at 5:00 p.m. after his tour had ended , he surveyed the road . The trooper didn'twrite a lot of tickets here , leaving that to the county deputies -- professional courtesy -- but he occasionally lit up somebody in a German or Italian car if he was in a mood , and this was the route he often took home at this time of day , so he knew the highway pretty well .
五点钟下班后，他开车回家，路上四下张望。这路段的交警不常开罚单，那些活儿通常留给县里的警员——大家都是同行，得给点面子嘛。不过要是心情不好，偶尔还是会找那些开德国车或意大利车的司机麻烦。这条路是他下班回家的常走路线，所以对这一带的路况了如指掌。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Heading home at 5:00 p.m. after his tour had ended , he surveyed the road . The trooper didn'twrite a lot of tickets here , leaving that to the county deputies -- professional courtesy -- but he occasionally lit up somebody in a German or Italian car if he was in a mood , and this was the route he often took home at this time of day , so he knew the highway pretty well .
下午5:00巡完逻后回家，他环视着道路。这里的交警不开很多罚单，把这事留给县里的副警官——这是职业上的相互尊重——但他偶尔会心情好时对开德国车或意大利车的人开张罚单，而这条路是他经常在这个时间回家的路线，所以他很熟悉这条高速公路。

source: The trooper thought about removing the cross , but the mourners might return to leave another one and endanger themselves again . Best just to leave it . Out of curiosity he 'd check with his sergeant in the morning and find out what had happened . He walked back to his car , tossed his hat on the seat and rubbed his crew cut . He pulled back into traffic , his mind no longer on roadside accidents . He was thinking about what his wife would be making for supper , about taking the kids to the pool afterward .
domestication: 士兵想着要不要把十字架拿走，但那些哀悼的人可能会再回来放一个，又把自己置于险境。还是留着吧。出于好奇，他打算早上回去问问中士是怎么回事。他走回车旁，把帽子扔在座位上，揉了揉自己的短发。重新汇入车流后，他的心思已不再放在路边的事故上。他想着妻子今晚会做些什么好吃的，想着待会儿带孩子们去游泳。
foreignization: 那个士兵考虑过移走十字架，但哀悼者可能会回来再放一个，从而再次危及自己。最好还是留着它。出于好奇，他会在早上和中士核实一下发生了什么事。他走回自己的车，把帽子扔在座位上，揉了揉他的平头。他重新驶入车流，心思已不再放在路边事故上。他在想妻子会为晚餐做什么，之后带孩子们去游泳池。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The trooper thought about removing the cross , but the mourners might return to leave another one and endanger themselves again . Best just to leave it . Out of curiosity he 'd check with his sergeant in the morning and find out what had happened . He walked back to his car , tossed his hat on the seat and rubbed his crew cut . He pulled back into traffic , his mind no longer on roadside accidents . He was thinking about what his wife would be making for supper , about taking the kids to the pool afterward .
士兵想着要不要把十字架拿走，但那些哀悼的人可能会再回来放一个，又把自己置于险境。还是留着吧。出于好奇，他打算早上回去问问中士是怎么回事。他走回车旁，把帽子扔在座位上，揉了揉自己的短发。重新汇入车流后，他的心思已不再放在路边的事故上。他想着妻子今晚会做些什么好吃的，想着待会儿带孩子们去游泳。

messages_foreignization.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

The trooper thought about removing the cross , but the mourners might return to leave another one and endanger themselves again . Best just to leave it . Out of curiosity he 'd check with his sergeant in the morning and find out what had happened . He walked back to his car , tossed his hat on the seat and rubbed his crew cut . He pulled back into traffic , his mind no longer on roadside accidents . He was thinking about what his wife would be making for supper , about taking the kids to the pool afterward .
那个士兵考虑过移走十字架，但哀悼者可能会回来再放一个，从而再次危及自己。最好还是留着它。出于好奇，他会在早上和中士核实一下发生了什么事。他走回自己的车，把帽子扔在座位上，揉了揉他的平头。他重新驶入车流，心思已不再放在路边事故上。他在想妻子会为晚餐做什么，之后带孩子们去游泳池。

source: But none of that mattered on Amber 's wedding day . It was the most perfect October day I ever hope to see . Rayne worked the hell out of it , too , swaying her hips slowly back and forth , as the light bounced off each and every sequin on that gown , hypnotizing and lulling people into the spell that was her . Rayne 's long locks were piled high on her head like a crown , and full , moist lips circled every syllable that came from her mouth .
她身穿白色服装，美得令人屏息。白色与她蓝黑色的皮肤相衬，显得神圣而神秘。雷恩也把这套衣服穿出了极致，她缓慢地左右摇摆着臀部，光线在礼服上的每一个亮片上反射，让人如痴如醉，陷入她所施的魔法之中。雷恩的长发高高地盘在头顶，如同一顶王冠，而她丰满湿润的双唇围绕着从口中吐出的每一个音节。

source: At least he could move into action now , thanks to that asinine European decision to give Russia command of the army 's secondary wing . But no , it had to be Russia , who was always so desperate for gold and land that there was no telling what their troops might do . domestication: 至少现在他可以行动了，多亏了欧洲那些蠢货把军队的次要翼指挥权给了俄国。他们干吗不给法国或意大利呢？这两个国家的野心可比俄国小多了。但不行，偏偏是俄国，这个国家对金银财宝和土地总是垂涎三尺，谁知道他们的部队会干出什么来。说不定一接到命令就冲进战场，抢个港口或者洗劫一座城市。真该死，他们的士兵多半都训练不足，纪律涣散。

source: Flower vendors hastily straightened their wares , while scared cabbies whipped up their horses and raced for safety , desperate to escape the ban on being caught in New Town by foreigners . They disappeared under the great barrel-vaulted colonnades , originally built to keep businesses above the great spring floods which regularly ripped through the entire valley , and into Old Town 's narrower , winding streets . Only the ancient floodwall protected the historic quarter , not broad avenues and squares , or deep underground drains .
domestication: 卖花的小贩们急忙整理起自己的货品，吓得出租车夫也抽打着马儿，拼命向安全的地方逃去，生怕被外国人抓到在新城里。他们消失在那大拱顶柱廊下，这些柱廊原本是为了抵御每年春天洪水侵袭整个山谷而建的，然后钻进了老城狭窄蜿蜒的小巷。只有那古老的防洪墙守护着历史区，没有宽阔的大道和广场，也没有深埋地下的排水系统。
foreignization: 卖花的小贩急忙整理他们的货物，而惊恐的出租车司机鞭策着马匹，急急忙忙地奔向安全地带，拼命想避免被外国人抓住在新城。他们消失在巨大的桶形拱廊下，这些拱廊最初是为了防止定期席卷整个山谷的大春汛淹没上方的商业区而建，然后进入了旧城更狭窄、曲折的街道。只有古老的防洪墙保护着历史街区，而不是宽阔的林荫大道和广场，或深埋地下的排水系统。

source: Crimson tile rooftops marked houses and shops clinging to the crag below the Citadel . Only a few , privileged newcomers would be permitted to ascend the narrow roads cut into the Iron Mountain and journey to the ducal palace , sprawled like a sated python over the rock . Black smoke slowly spiraled into the sky behind the cathedral 's gothic spires , as it had for all the millennia since swords were first beaten out against the peak 's unyielding heart .
domestication: 红瓦屋顶点缀着城堡下方峭壁上的房屋和商铺。只有少数几位新来的有特权的人才能踏上铁山上的狭窄道路，前往那座像吃饱了的蟒蛇一样横卧在岩石上的公爵宫殿。大教堂哥特式的尖顶后面，黑烟缓缓升腾，千百年来，自从第一把剑在这坚硬的山心里锤炼成形，这烟就从未停过。
foreignization: 猩红色的瓦片屋顶标记着依附在城堡下方岩壁上的房屋和商店。只有少数受到优待的新来者会被允许沿着铁山中凿出的狭窄道路攀登，并前往像一条吃饱了的蟒蛇般横卧在岩石上的公爵宫殿。黑色的烟雾缓缓地从哥特式尖塔后升起，就像自剑首次在这座山峰不可动摇的心脏处锻造以来的数千年一样。

source: Hunter stood before the polished plaintiff desk to the judge 's right , still wearing his sharp , pin-stripe suit coat despite the heat of the packed courtroom . Even though the four attorneys at the defense desk had removed their coats hours ago when the judge said it was all right to do so . For some time he 'd been gazing down at a single piece of paper lying on the desk , as though hypnotized by the typed words on it and the heavy , unique signature beneath the words . Finally he brought his dark , penetrating eyes to those of the white-haired judge .
domestication: 猎人站在法官右侧那光亮的原告桌前，尽管法庭里挤得像蒸笼，他还是穿着那身笔挺的条纹西装。虽然被告桌上的四位律师早在法官说可以脱掉外套时就照做了。他盯着桌上的一张纸看了好一会儿，仿佛被上面打字的文字和下面那个沉甸甸、独一无二的签名迷住了。终于，他那深邃锐利的目光对上了白发法官的眼睛。

source: It was Hunter 's father who had decided what his career would be early on in his childhood , even before Hunter really knew what a lawyer was . Robert Hunter Lee would be an attorney , his father would announce every evening as the family sat down to dinner . A New York City litigator , he 'd specify during the meal . Case closed , he 'd say over dessert . Night after night , as far back as Hunter could remember .

source: The judge , too , Hunter noticed . Which fit . He 'd been worried at the start of the trial that a Montana judge might make it difficult for a New York lawyer carrying a big reputation into his courtroom , but that had n't turned out to be the case at all . The man in black had been completely fair , which Hunter had found was true about most Montanans . They were tough -- because Montana was a tough place to live -- but they were fair . Which was refreshing . It might help with the size of the award , too .
domestication: 法官也在场，亨特留意到了。这在意料之中。一开始他还担心，蒙大拿的法官可能会给这位带着显赫名声来到他们法庭的纽约律师找麻烦，但事实证明，这种担忧完全是多余的。穿黑袍的法官非常公正，这一点让亨特感到，大多数蒙大拿人都这样。这里的人硬气，因为蒙大拿本身就是一个让人不得不硬气的地方，但他们公平。这真让人眼前一亮。或许，这也对赔偿金额的大小有好处。
foreignization: 法官也注意到了，亨特注意到。这很符合情况。他在审判开始时担心，一位蒙大拿州的法官可能会让一位带着显赫名声进入他法庭的纽约律师感到困难，但事实证明并非如此。穿黑衣服的人非常公正，亨特发现大多数蒙大拿人都如此。他们很坚强——因为蒙大拿是一个生活艰难的地方——但他们很公平。这让人感到耳目一新。这也可能有助于赔偿金额的大小。

source: " Into a monster . " Hunter 's voice resounded throughout the courtroom . " A monster seeking water anywhere it could find it because that 's what anhydrous ammonia does . It sucks water out of everything it comes into contact with . Like it did from the skin , lungs and eyes of those unlucky people in its path that terrible spring day in Fort Mason . " Hunter pointed at the children in the front row . " Like it did from the eyes of these innocent children , blinding them as their lenses dehydrated like puddles beneath the Sahara sun . "

“四个人在煤气将他们的肺烤得骨干时死亡，十人失明，包括这些坐在我面前的可怜的孩子。幸运的是，他们只是单眼失明。”猎人的表情变得悲伤，他摇了摇头。“我不敢相信我刚刚说了‘幸运的是’。我很抱歉，”他低声说道，严肃地点了点头，向那个小男孩和女孩，然后是他们的父母致意。“非常抱歉。”

source: Then , lying on the bed , he thought about the zigzag trail he had cut across the country after leaving New York City . Up to see his sister in Albany , down to Phoenix , Arizona , to visit his mother , who had moved there from upstate New York following the death of Tony 's father . On to Las Vegas , on again , driving a few hours every day from place to place with no destination in mind , just driving with the thought that he would know it when he saw it . He had arrived in Portland , Oregon , with that vague thought still in his head and a bit of regret that he had not yet found it , a place where he wanted to stay and stop the insane driving .
domestication: 躺在床上，他回想着离开纽约后在各地留下的曲折足迹。先是去了阿尔巴尼看望姐姐，又南下亚利桑那州的菲尼克斯探望母亲，她是在托尼的父亲去世后从纽约上州搬过去的。接着到了拉斯维加斯，继续上路，每天开几个小时的车，没有目的地，心里想着到了就知道了。最后来到了俄勒冈州的波特兰，那种模糊的想法还在，但也带着点遗憾，还没找到想要停留的地方，想让这疯狂的旅程停下来。
foreignization: 然后，躺在床上，他回想着离开纽约市后在国家上留下的曲折轨迹。去奥尔巴尼看望他的姐姐，下到亚利桑那州的菲尼克斯去看望他的母亲，她是在托尼的父亲去世后从纽约上州搬到那里的。接着去了拉斯维加斯，再次上路，每天开车几个小时，从一个地方到另一个地方，没有明确的目的地，只是开车，心里想着当他看到时就会知道。他带着这种模糊的想法来到了俄勒冈州的波特兰，并且有些遗憾还没有找到它，一个他想停留并停止疯狂驾驶的地方。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Then , lying on the bed , he thought about the zigzag trail he had cut across the country after leaving New York City . Up to see his sister in Albany , down to Phoenix , Arizona , to visit his mother , who had moved there from upstate New York following the death of Tony 's father . On to Las Vegas , on again , driving a few hours every day from place to place with no destination in mind , just driving with the thought that he would know it when he saw it . He had arrived in Portland , Oregon , with that vague thought still in his head and a bit of regret that he had not yet found it , a place where he wanted to stay and stop the insane driving .

source: Strictly speaking , she was n't really looking at the Gulf of Mexico . Faye was n't sure how to name this water . In south Louisiana , the land just drifts to sea . The water at Faye 's feet was connected to a bunch of canals and island-dotted estuaries and grassy coves that extended south and west until they eventually connected to Barataria Bay , and it was connected to the Gulf of Mexico . Regardless of its name , this water smelled like the gulf breezes that blew in her bedroom window every morning .
domestication: 严格说来，她并不是在看墨西哥湾。菲耶不确定这片水域该叫什么名字。在路易斯安那州南部，陆地渐渐没入海中。菲耶脚下的水，连着一条条运河，还有布满小岛的河口和长满草的海湾，一直往南往西延伸，最终汇入巴塔里亚湾，再与墨西哥湾相连。不管叫什么名字，这片水散发出的气味，就像每天早晨吹进卧室的海风一样熟悉。
foreignization: 严格地说，她并不是在看墨西哥湾。费伊不确定该如何称呼这片水域。在路易斯安那州南部，土地逐渐向海洋延伸。费伊脚下的水与许多运河、岛屿点缀的河口和草滩相连，向南和向西延伸，最终与巴塔里亚湾相连，而它又与墨西哥湾相连。不管叫什么名字，这片水散发出每天早晨从她卧室窗户吹进来的海湾微风的味道。

messages_domestication.content:
Translate the following text to Chinese.

Strictly speaking , she was n't really looking at the Gulf of Mexico . Faye was n't sure how to name this water . In south Louisiana , the land just drifts to sea . The water at Faye 's feet was connected to a bunch of canals and island-dotted estuaries and grassy coves that extended south and west until they eventually connected to Barataria Bay , and it was connected to the Gulf of Mexico . Regardless of its name , this water smelled like the gulf breezes that blew in her bedroom window every morning .
严格说来，她并不是在看墨西哥湾。菲耶不确定这片水域该叫什么名字。在路易斯安那州南部，陆地渐渐没入海中。菲耶脚下的水，连着一条条运河，还有布满小岛的河口和长满草的海湾，一直往南往西延伸，最终汇入巴塔里亚湾，再与墨西哥湾相连。不管叫什么名字，这片水散发出的气味，就像每天早晨吹进卧室的海风一样熟悉。

source: Maybe he 'd loved her and maybe he had n't , but Steve Daigle was not the kind of man who stayed around for the hard times . Justine 's diagnosis of terminal cancer would ordinarily have sent him out the door and on his way to the next parish , but not in this case . He had an inheritance to consider . Justine had owned a piece of her late father 's houseboat , not to mention a piece of a nice little pile of stock in the oil company where he 'd worked .

source: After he 'd found out about that inheritance , it had taken him a while to understand why Justine could n't just go back to her hometown and kick her stepmother off that houseboat . Finally , he 'd called a cousin who 'd almost finished paralegal school , and she 'd walked him through Louisiana 's twisty inheritance laws . source: Others would be hurrying to assess the other aspects of the area -- the plants , the animals , the towns , the roads , the economy , the air quality , the water quality -- but Faye just needed to focus on its archaeology : the physical remnants of human history near the mouth of the Mississippi . source: Chapter Two Hebert Demeray missed his old hangouts , the ones that had washed away during Hurricane Katrina . He missed the way beer bottles stuck to the tops of their bars , grimy with old shellac and spilled bourbon . Nostalgia gripped him when he remembered worn floorboards so uneven that the bathroom doors scraped and squeaked and sometimes did n't close all the way . He even missed the stench of old urinals , served by plumbing that had rebelled after years of carrying an overload of beer piss and vomit .
domestication: 第二章 赫伯特·德梅雷想念他的老据点，那些在卡特里娜飓风中被冲走的地方。他怀念那些酒瓶粘在吧台上，上面满是陈旧的清漆和洒落的波旁威士忌。一想起那些破旧的地板，高低不平，厕所门刮擦着地面，吱吱作响，有时甚至关不上，他就感到一阵怀旧之情涌上心头。就连那些老旧尿槽散发出的恶臭，他也开始怀念了，那些下水道经过数年超负荷运转，运载了无数啤酒尿液和呕吐物后，终于罢工了。
foreignization: 第二章 赫伯特·德梅雷怀念他以前常去的地方，那些在卡特里娜飓风中被冲走的地方。他怀念啤酒瓶粘在吧台上，上面覆盖着旧的清漆和洒出的波旁威士忌。当他回想起那些磨损的地板板时，怀旧情绪笼罩着他，这些地板板如此不平，以至于浴室门会刮擦和吱嘎作响，有时甚至无法完全关上。他甚至怀念那些老旧小便池的恶臭，它们的下水道在多年承载过量的啤酒尿液和呕吐物后终于罢工了。

source: Hebert thought of his mother , as Cajun men will do when under the influence of booze and nostalgia . He had n't spoken to her in years , not since the last time she cursed him and cast him out . She had n't lived at home in almost four years , since going away to Exeter .
“我才不像郊区来的呢。”亚历克莎抗议道。她瞥了一眼旁边几个瘦削的欧洲小伙子，他们正抽着烟，聊着天，拿着手机。挺帅的，但可能不是她的菜？“我爸住在曼彻斯特。”她差点说，“我住在曼彻斯特”，但她再也没把那个她长大的大房子当成家了，尤其是自从老爸娶了那个拜金空姐贝琳达之后。她已经快四年没在家住了，从去埃克塞特上学开始。

我的左食指沿着裤子口袋外侧勾勒出小吗啡瓶的形状。自上次服药以来已经过去了将近两个小时。尽管我右手的疼痛是可以忍受的，而且我的头脑仍然沉浸在鸦片带来的美妙模糊中，但在过去的十五分钟里，我一直在与自己斗争——在莫雷蒂出现之前再尝一口？我可能有一段时间没有机会了。但我不能服用太多。我必须保持清醒。

source: Movement on the sidewalk down the block caught my attention , and my hand went to the .32 tucked into my belt . I pressed farther back into the shadows of the alley , squinting at the couple who had just turned the corner . The few streetlamps that worked were dim and widely spaced , doing little to add to the meager glow spilling from the windows of the crumbling redbrick buildings .
domestication: 街对面人行道上的动静吸引了我的注意，手不由自主地摸向腰间的那把点三二手枪。我往巷子的阴影深处缩了缩，眯着眼打量刚转过街角的那对男女。几盏还能亮的路灯昏暗且间隔很远，几乎没能给这破败的红砖楼间添上多少光亮。

source: I could n't get worked up over every man who passed by . This was a busy area -- a run-down , though typical , slice of Detroit 's Little Italy . I was plenty familiar with the scenery here , after investigating Vito Adamo 's Black Hand gang for the last few months . Even though Adamo had n't been directly responsible for the death of my friend , Wesley McRae , he had helped . That was enough . I pulled out my watch and angled it toward a streetlamp -- twelve thirty . Moretti should be here .
domestication: 我可不能对每个路过的男人上心。这地方人来人往，算是底特律小意大利区的典型破落一角。过去几个月里，为了调查维托·阿达莫的黑手党团伙，我对这里的一草一木都熟得不能再熟了。虽然阿达莫不是直接害死我朋友韦斯利·麦克雷的凶手，但他确实出了力。这就够了。我掏出怀表，对着路灯一瞧——十二点半。莫雷蒂该到了。
foreignization: 我不能对每个路过的男人激动起来。这是一个繁忙的地区——底特律小意大利的一个破旧但典型的区域。在过去的几个月里调查维托·亚当诺的黑手党团伙后，我对这里的景色非常熟悉。即使亚当诺没有直接导致我的朋友韦斯利·麦克雷的死亡，他也帮忙了。这已经足够了。我掏出怀表，对着街灯倾斜——十二点半。莫雷蒂应该到了。

source: I pulled off my derby and the handkerchief I 'd tied around my head and I wiped the sweat from my face . Past midnight and still somewhere near ninety degrees . For the tenth time tonight , I slipped the handkerchief back over my head and spun it around to cover my face below my eyes -- to be sure it would stay in place . If Moretti recognized me , I 'd have to kill him . I did n't want to do that . After shifting the mask around to the back again , I returned my derby to my head and settled in to wait . I needed a cigarette but restrained myself -- it would give away my position .
domestication: 我摘下圆顶礼帽，解开绑在头上的手帕，擦了擦脸上的汗。都过了午夜，这天还热得跟蒸笼似的，差不多有四十度。今晚第十次了，我又把手帕往头上一搭，扭来扭去，盖住眼睛以下的脸，确保它不会掉下来。要是莫雷蒂认出我，我就得动手干掉他。可我不想那样做。我又把手帕往后调了调，重新戴上帽子，坐下来耐心等待。真想抽根烟，但还是忍住了——那会暴露我的位置。

source: I pulled the Colt pistol from my belt and checked the load -- seven bullets I hoped I would n't need tonight . I cocked it , flicked on the safety , and stuffed it back into my belt . At one thirty I crossed the street and entered the dark stairwell . The mews of kittens came from a crate in the corner . Trickles of light filtered in from the hallway , illuminating the steps to vague dark shapes . The stair rail was sticky , the air wet , smelling of mold and sewage . Muffled voices rose and fell as I crept up to the second-floor landing . I leaned out over the rail and looked above me . No one stood guard . Moretti did n't rate his boss 's protection .
domestication: 我从腰间拔出柯尔特手枪，检查了一下弹匣——七发子弹，但愿今晚用不上。我拉了拉枪栓，打开保险，又塞回腰带里。凌晨一点半，我穿过街道，走进了漆黑的楼梯间。角落里的箱子传来小猫的叫声。走廊里透进几缕微弱的光线，勉强照亮了模糊的台阶。楼梯扶手粘糊糊的，空气又潮又湿，弥漫着霉味和下水道的臭气。我轻手轻脚地爬到二楼平台，探头向下看了看。没人守着，莫雷蒂还没资格享受老板的特别保护。

Though they did n't sound like they 'd be a threat , I had nowhere to hide , and certainly no explanation for lurking on the steps . domestication: 下面传来一阵脚步声，一男一女摇摇晃晃地走上楼梯，含糊不清的话语和醉醺醺的笑声在楼梯间回荡。虽然听上去他们没啥威胁，但我无处可躲，更没法解释自己为什么在这儿鬼鬼祟祟。心想着他们最好能在二楼停下来，我索性往台阶上一躺，装作睡着了。在这栋楼里，喝得酩酊大醉的人在楼梯间打盹儿，应该也不是什么稀罕事儿。

I turned to leave and saw a spray of red on the dingy ivory wall at the side of the bed . I took a step toward it , and another . Near the wall , the blanket was spattered with dark stains . Now I saw a form -- a naked man lying facedown , jammed between the bed and the wall . I pulled the handkerchief down around my neck and leaned in .
我转身要走，眼角却瞥见床边脏兮兮的象牙白墙上有一抹鲜红。我往前走了几步，越靠近越看清了。墙边的毯子上溅满了深色的斑点。这时我才注意到有个身影——一个赤裸的男人趴着，卡在床和墙之间。我拉下脖子上的手帕，凑近了一看。

source: As I thought , I massaged my dead right hand with my left , an unconscious habit I 'd picked up shortly after I left the hospital . I was n't sure if it was just a nervous tic or if , somewhere deep down , I thought if I massaged it enough , the pain would stop . I watched the fingers spread apart and then close halfway into a fist . A new wave of pain shot like lightning up my arm .
domestication: 果然，我用左手揉着那只半死不活的右手，这是从出院后不久就养成的习惯。我也分不清这到底是紧张的小动作，还是潜意识里觉得多揉一揉，疼痛就能缓解。看着手指张开又半握成拳，一阵新的痛感如同闪电般沿着手臂窜了上来。

source: I looked up at the sky and mouthed , I 'm sorry , Wes . He was the best friend I could have ever had , repeatedly risking his life and finally giving it -- for me , a man who had disdained him for his homosexuality . I shook my head . source: The most glittering of the English boarders was Celia , who could often be heard on the telephone under the stairs , expressing disbelief : " Tell me you did n't . Are you completely barking mad , poppet ? He ca n't have . The road was empty and she could n't see anyone around , so she got out of the car and opened the passenger-side door , which faced the tree line . Lila stepped out of her boots and work pants and for a second , exposed the naked body she was still proud of . She slipped the black dress over her head , the soft fabric sliding down her back and falling into place .

source: As a type , if only at first glance , he was simply an honest fellow and an enthusiast . But life has a way of pinning labels on us . Life is crude . It forces us to define ourselves more narrowly . And that 's why at his research institute the aging Victor Sushkov came across as that familiar type we aU know from the ' 60s and ' 70s . He was , as everyone around used to joke , a " Champion of Human Rights , " running up and down the corridors . Once he 'd been a Komsomol stalwart . Once a trade union activist . And now he was this nice , spry , little old guy .
domestication: 从表面上看，他就是一个实诚人，一个热情分子。可生活总爱给我们贴标签，生活粗鲁得很，逼着我们把自己定义得越来越狭窄。所以，在他的研究所里，上了年纪的维克多·苏什科夫就像咱们都熟悉的老一辈，六七十年代的典型人物。周围的人都开玩笑说他是“人权斗士”，整天在走廊里跑上跑下的。他曾是共青社的骨干，工会活动的积极分子。现在成了个和蔼可亲、精精神神的小老头儿。
foreignization: 作为一类人，如果仅从第一眼来看，他只是一个诚实的家伙和一个热衷者。但生活有一种给我们贴标签的方式。生活是粗鲁的。它迫使我们更狭隘地定义自己。这就是为什么在他的研究所里，老去的维克多·苏什科夫给人的印象是我们大家都知道的60年代和70年代的那种熟悉类型。他就像周围的人常开玩笑说的那样，是一个“人权捍卫者”，在走廊上跑来跑去。他曾是一个共青团的骨干。曾是一个工会活动家。而现在他是一个可爱、灵活的小老头。

作为一类人，如果仅从第一眼来看，他只是一个诚实的家伙和一个热衷者。但生活有一种给我们贴标签的方式。生活是粗鲁的。它迫使我们更狭隘地定义自己。这就是为什么在他的研究所里，老去的维克多·苏什科夫给人的印象是我们大家都知道的60年代和70年代的那种熟悉类型。他就像周围的人常开玩笑说的那样，是一个“人权捍卫者”，在走廊上跑来跑去。他曾是一个共青团的骨干。曾是一个工会活动家。而现在他是一个可爱、灵活的小老头。

source: Since it was the boss himself who decided who would stay for this simple work and who would not , the girls were fully dependent on him and he made full use of it . Once in two years the gloomy , solitary Odintsov changed his contingent and once again made the most of it . A smooth operator ! It 's curious that he carried out all this casually , almost unwillingly . With a gloomy face , and quietly . That 's the kind of man he was . He bedded one , he bedded another . For some reason he would begin with the most timid , homely one . Or else he bedded two at once , diversifying the week . Yet , it seems , also without passion . Also calmly . Just so his itch got scratched . ( Well , you ca n't have great love every day . )
domestication: 既然决定谁留下干这轻松活儿的是老板本人，姑娘们自然全得看他脸色行事，而他也正好借此机会大做文章。每隔两年，一向阴沉孤独的奥丁佐夫就会换一批人手，再次充分利用这个机会。真是个老油条！奇怪的是，他做这一切时似乎漫不经心，甚至有些不情愿。一脸阴郁，悄无声息。他就是这种人。今天上了这个，明天又上那个。不知为何，他总是从最胆小、长相平平的那个开始。或者干脆一次来两个，让一周的生活多点花样。不过，好像也没有什么激情。一切都是那么平静。只是满足一下自己的欲望罢了。（好吧，天天都那么热烈也不现实。）
foreignization: 既然决定谁留下做这项简单工作、谁不留下的是老板本人，女孩们就完全依赖于他，而他也充分利用了这一点。每隔两年，阴郁孤独的奥丁佐夫就会更换一批手下，再次充分利用这种情况。真是个老手！奇怪的是，他做这一切都显得漫不经心，几乎是不太情愿的样子。板着脸，悄无声息地。他就是这种人。他和一个上床，又和另一个上床。不知为何，他总是从最胆小、最不起眼的那个开始。或者他会同时和两个上床，以此来调剂一周的生活。然而，似乎也是没有激情的。也是冷静地。只是为了让他的痒处得到满足。（好吧，你不可能每天都有伟大的爱情。）

Just so his itch got scratched . ( Well , you ca n't have great love every day . )

source: But there was something else they used to talk about . Something amazing . Both men anticipated their own longevity . They discovered this unexpectedly . As it turned out , their grandmothers and grandfathers had lived to a hundred . Well , what do you think , does n't this obUge one to live on ! Does n't this inspire one ? And so even now , in retirement , the lives of both Victors were in no way ending , their whole long lives were still ahead .
domestication: 但还有一件事他们经常提起，那可真是了不得。两人都不约而同地发现自己可能会长寿，这发现纯属意外。原来他们的祖辈，爷爷奶奶，都活到了一百岁。你说说看，这不是逼着人要活得长久吗？这不是让人干劲十足吗？所以啊，即使现在退休了，两个维克多的生活也一点没有结束的迹象，他们觉得漫长的人生还在前方等着呢。
foreignization: 但是他们过去还谈论过其他事情。一些令人惊讶的事情。两人都预期自己会长寿。这是他们意外发现的。结果，他们的祖母和祖父都活到了一百岁。那么，你认为呢，这难道不迫使一个人继续活下去吗！这难道不令人振奋吗？因此，即使现在退休了，两个维克多的生活也丝毫没有结束，他们漫长的一生还在前方。

source: And the playful " gifts " they gave one another ! The wills ! It really was a kind of passionate game . The intellectual sport of idle old men . This sophisticated ritual unfolded picturesquely : both Victors , immensely proud , would exchange " papers , " texts they had written themselves . From hand to hand , elegantly . With a witness , perhaps myself , these texts were read aloud . ( When you know you 'll five a hundred years , leaving a will is a great joy , no doubt . ) Out loud by a fire . With pleasure . With satisfaction . Repeating key words . Slippery legal terms . Convoluted sentences . All the jibberish of a notary 's office .
domestication: 他们互赠的那些逗趣的“礼物”！那些遗嘱！这真是一种充满激情的游戏，老人们的闲暇智力游戏。这个精致的仪式进行得非常生动：两位维克托先生都极为自豪，互相交换自己写的“文件”，文雅地传到对方手中。有时我也会在场作见证，这些文件会被大声朗读出来。（既然知道能活一百年，立遗嘱自然是一件乐事。）炉火旁，他们带着愉悦和满足感朗读，重复着关键的词句，那些滑溜的法律术语，绕口的句子，全是公证处的那一套废话。
foreignization: 而他们彼此赠送的那些充满戏谑的“礼物”！那些遗嘱！这确实是一种充满激情的游戏。这是无所事事的老人们的一种智力运动。这一精致的仪式以一种生动的方式展开：两位维克多，极其自豪地交换“文件”，他们自己写下的文字。手递手，优雅地。或许有见证人，可能是我，这些文字被大声朗读。（当你知道你将活一百年时，留下遗嘱无疑是一种极大的乐趣。）在炉火旁大声朗读。带着愉悦。带着满足。重复关键词。滑稽的法律术语。复杂的句子。所有公证处的胡言乱语。

And the playful " gifts " they gave one another ! The wills ! It really was a kind of passionate game . The intellectual sport of idle old men . This sophisticated ritual unfolded picturesquely : both Victors , immensely proud , would exchange " papers , " texts they had written themselves . From hand to hand , elegantly . With a witness , perhaps myself , these texts were read aloud . ( When you know you 'll five a hundred years , leaving a will is a great joy , no doubt . ) Out loud by a fire . With pleasure . With satisfaction . Repeating key words . Slippery legal terms . Convoluted sentences . All the jibberish of a notary 's office .
他们互赠的那些逗趣的“礼物”！那些遗嘱！这真是一种充满激情的游戏，老人们的闲暇智力游戏。这个精致的仪式进行得非常生动：两位维克托先生都极为自豪，互相交换自己写的“文件”，文雅地传到对方手中。有时我也会在场作见证，这些文件会被大声朗读出来。（既然知道能活一百年，立遗嘱自然是一件乐事。）炉火旁，他们带着愉悦和满足感朗读，重复着关键的词句，那些滑溜的法律术语，绕口的句子，全是公证处的那一套废话。

source: But now the plot began to unfold : Victor 's sister , an exemplary , respectable lady , a high school teacher , noticed a familiar handwriting that pricked her heart . A particular slope of the letters that she recognized from childhood . She read it through ? oh , so that 's what it was ! Victor , her elder brother , in his right mind and of sound memory , wrote that after his death he willed to be buried in his native Orenburzhe , in the ruined village where his grandfathers each lived to a hundred , and where for ages and ages since , there had been no houses or walls or even chimneys sticking out forlornly . But still there was a cemetery . For the time being . The remains of an old cemetery , which he , Victor , had visited with great interest about two years before .

Victor , her elder brother , in his right mind and of sound memory , wrote that after his death he willed to be buried in his native Orenburzhe , in the ruined village where his grandfathers each lived to a hundred , and where for ages and ages since , there had been no houses or walls or even chimneys sticking out forlornly . But still there was a cemetery . For the time being . The remains of an old cemetery , which he , Victor , had visited with great interest about two years before .

但事情很快有了眉目：维克多的姐姐，一个端庄体面的女人，高中老师，她发现了一笔熟悉的字迹，心猛地一颤。那些字母特有的倾斜角度，她从小就认得。她一口气读完了——哦，原来是这么回事！维克多，她的哥哥，头脑清醒，记忆清晰，在遗嘱中写道，希望死后能埋在老家奥伦堡，那个已经荒废的村庄，祖辈们在那里都活到了一百岁。可如今，那里早已没有了房屋、院墙，甚至连烟囱也消失了，只剩下一片荒凉。不过，那里还有一个墓地，暂时还保留着。那是块古老的墓地，两年前，维克多还特意去参观过，兴趣盎然。

But still there was a cemetery . For the time being . The remains of an old cemetery , which he , Victor , had visited with great interest about two years before .

source: MUCH LIKE ANY CLOSE RELATIONSHIP , the intimacy of a chamber ensemble requires open lines of communication . The added complexity is , however , being able to understand , give , and heed attenuated and nonverbal cues while performing . Invariably , any group wil ! develop a system for communicating with cues . " Most often it is the first violin that is responsible for this , although there are many moments when the music demands that one or more of the other players take that responsibility , " Juilliard String Quartet violisi Samuel Rhodes says . " There are more subtle ways of signaling than outright cueing .
domestication: 就像任何亲密的关系一样，室内乐组合的默契也需要开放的沟通。不过，难就难在表演时还能理解、给予并留意那些细微的、非语言的暗示。不管怎样，每个小组都会发展出一套沟通系统。“通常是由第一小提琴手负责，但音乐有时会要求其他一位或几位演奏者来承担这个责任，”朱莉亚弦乐四重奏的中提琴手塞缪尔·罗德斯说。“除了直接给出提示，还有一些更加微妙的信号方式。”

source: Cues are essential in helping to keep an ensemble together during performance , but catching them can sometimes be difficult . A nod or a shoulder raise - sometimes simply breathing is a cue . " Shaping notes , vibrato , a shift of posture are all cues , " violinist Karen Kirn of the Grammy Award-winning Parker Quartet says .

source: But years ago I married Alexandra Markopoulos . Xan is skinny and looks frail . She is n't actually frail . She 's wiry and indestructible but dark and short and small . And I 'm the lanky sort with knobby knees . I was a runner once in high school and for a while in college . I was n't Xan 's type either . She preferred short men she could look directly in the eye , men with meat on their bones . I 'd wrap myself around her skinniness and yearn for more hip , more bosom , more weight . I 'm sure even then she missed some things in me too .

source: I like to imagine my defunct marriage on Worldtime . The boundary moves back and forth in the disputed territory between Christopher and Alexandra . Matthew breaks off and federates with Vanessa Hu . Emma keeps forming shaky alliances with an Ethan or a Noah . I could provide sidebars , but where to start ? I 'd have to go back at least to my first birthday , the week we dropped the A bombs on Japan . I was a war baby , my father was still in the Pacific . There were only my mother and aunt and grandmother at home , my women . As soon as you 're born you start learning things .
domestication: 我喜欢在我的婚姻废墟上畅想时空。克里斯托弗和亚历山德拉之间，那片争议的领地，界线时而前移时而后退。马修则脱离出去，与凡妮莎·胡结盟。艾玛总是摇摆不定，时而和以坦或诺亚结成脆弱的同盟。我可以把这些侧枝末节讲出来，但该从哪里开始呢？至少得回到我的第一个生日，那时我们刚刚在日本投下原子弹。我是战争的产物，父亲还在太平洋战场上。家里只有母亲、姨妈和祖母，我的女人们。人一出生就开始学习了。

source: I 'm watching Vi 's white plastic fork dip down , stab , and rise again to her smoothly kissable unlipsticked lips . " I should 've , " I say . " I never thought it actually would . " So I reel off some of Xan 's quibbles , how I did n't separate the recyclables correctly and rolled up the windowshades unevenly and mixed soup spoons with dessert spoons in the drawer . Not that any of that was really it , but I do n't intend to go into the worse stuff .
domestication: 我看着维的白色塑料叉子一上一下，叉起食物送到她那光滑、没涂口红、亲起来一定很舒服的嘴唇边。“我应该的，”我说，“我只是没想到真的会发生。”于是，我就开始列举些桑的一些抱怨，比如我没有正确地分类回收垃圾，卷起窗帘时没有对齐，还把汤匙和甜点勺混在了一起放在抽屉里。其实这些都不是真正的原因，但我可不想说那些更糟糕的事。

source: In bed I relax with the clicker and the TV . I do n't stay long on any channel unless my favorite shows are on , which they are n't - Amy Sedaris , Tracy Ullman , Sarah Silverman , shows with nutty ladies . When I was a kid I watched " Our Miss Brooks , " " My Little Margie , " " I Married Joan " - I 'd like to see those old shows again to find out what used to make me laugh .
domestication: 躺在床上，我拿着遥控器看起了电视。除非是我喜欢的节目，否则我不会在一个频道停留太久，但我喜欢的那些节目——比如艾米·塞达里斯、特雷西·乌尔曼、莎拉·西尔弗曼主演的，这些疯癫女士的节目——现在都播不了。小时候，我常看《我们的布罗克斯小姐》《我的小玛吉》《我娶了琼》，真想再看看这些老剧，回味一下当年让我捧腹大笑的情节。

source: In the dressing room , the composer takes off his shoes and socks . The smell is powerful . He has a theory about sweat . Another about photographers . Another about Germany .
